Now let me create `src/lib/offers.ts` with the value equation scoring, anatomy validation, and diagnostic loop.

**tool `write`** completed
```input
{
  "content": "/**\n * Offers — value equation scoring, anatomy validation, diagnostic loop.\n * Based on the \"offers\" skill from coreyhaines31/marketingskills (Alex Hormozi $100M Offers framework).\n */\n\nimport { db, uid, now } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nexport type BusinessType = \"service\" | \"course\" | \"coaching\" | \"info\" | \"saas\" | \"agency\" | \"b2b\";\n\nexport interface Offer {\n  id: string;\n  workspace_id: string;\n  name: string;\n  business_type: BusinessType;\n  core_deliverable: string | null;\n  bonus_stack: string | null;\n  guarantee: string | null;\n  scarcity: string | null;\n  offer_name: string | null;\n  price: string | null;\n  payment_structure: string | null;\n  status: string;\n  notes: string | null;\n  created_at: number;\n  updated_at: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ValueEquationScore {\n  id: string;\n  offer_id: string;\n  dream_outcome: number;\n  perceived_likelihood: number;\n  time_delay: number;\n  effort_sacrifice: number;\n  computed_value: number;\n  binding_constraint: string;\n  notes: string | null;\n  created_at: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ValueEquationInput {\n  dream_outcome: number;\n  perceived_likelihood: number;\n  time_delay: number;\n  effort_sacrifice: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ValueEquationResult {\n  value: number;\n  binding_constraint: string;\n  leverScores: { lever: string; score: number; direction: \"up\" | \"down\" }[];\n  recommendation: string;\n}\n\nconst LEVER_NAMES: Record<keyof ValueEquationInput, { label: string; direction: \"up\" | \"down\" }> = {\n  dream_outcome: { label: \"Dream Outcome\", direction: \"up\" },\n  perceived_likelihood: { label: \"Perceived Likelihood\", direction: \"up\" },\n  time_delay: { label: \"Time Delay\", direction: \"down\" },\n  effort_sacrifice: { label: \"Effort & Sacrifice\", direction: \"down\" },\n};\n\n/**\n * Value Equation: (Dream × Likelihood) / (Time × Effort)\n * Scores 1-10 each. Value = numerator / denominator, scaled 0-100.\n */\nexport function scoreValueEquation(input: ValueEquationInput): ValueEquationResult {\n  const clamped = {\n    dream_outcome: Math.max(1, Math.min(10, input.dream_outcome)),\n    perceived_likelihood: Math.max(1, Math.min(10, input.perceived_likelihood)),\n    time_delay: Math.max(1, Math.min(10, input.time_delay)),\n    effort_sacrifice: Math.max(1, Math.min(10, input.effort_sacrifice)),\n  };\n\n  const numerator = clamped.dream_outcome * clamped.perceived_likelihood;\n  const denominator = clamped.time_delay * clamped.effort_sacrifice;\n  const rawValue = denominator > 0 ? numerator / denominator : 0;\n  const value = Math.round((rawValue / 100) * 10000) / 100;\n\n  const leverScores = (Object.keys(LEVER_NAMES) as Array<keyof ValueEquationInput>).map((k) => ({\n    lever: LEVER_NAMES[k].label,\n    score: clamped[k],\n    direction: LEVER_NAMES[k].direction,\n  }));\n\n  const minUp = Math.min(clamped.dream_outcome, clamped.perceived_likelihood);\n  const maxDown = Math.max(clamped.time_delay, clamped.effort_sacrifice);\n  let bindingConstraint: string;\n  let recommendation: string;\n\n  if (minUp <= maxDown) {\n    if (clamped.dream_outcome <= clamped.perceived_likelihood) {\n      bindingConstraint = \"Dream Outcome\";\n      recommendation = \"Connect the offer to a bigger goal. Name the dream outcome specifically — not the feature, the transformation.\";\n    } else {\n      bindingConstraint = \"Perceived Likelihood\";\n      recommendation = \"Add proof: named case studies, specific data, methodology. Make the 'how it works' concrete enough to believe.\";\n    }\n  } else {\n    if (clamped.time_delay >= clamped.effort_sacrifice) {\n      bindingConstraint = \"Time Delay\";\n      recommendation = \"Shorten time-to-first-result. Faster onboarding, faster first win, compress the timeline.\";\n    } else {\n      bindingConstraint = \"Effort & Sacrifice\";\n      recommendation = \"Reduce customer effort. Done-for-you, simpler process, fewer decisions, lower learning curve.\";\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { value, bindingConstraint: bindingConstraint, leverScores, recommendation };\n}\n\nexport interface OfferAnatomyResult {\n  complete: boolean;\n  components: Array<{ component: string; question: string; present: boolean; value: string | null }>;\n  missing: string[];\n  summary: string;\n}\n\nconst ANATOMY_COMPONENTS: Array<{ key: keyof Pick<Offer, \"core_deliverable\" | \"bonus_stack\" | \"guarantee\" | \"scarcity\" | \"offer_name\" | \"price\">; label: string; question: string }> = [\n  { key: \"core_deliverable\", label: \"Core Deliverable\", question: \"What do they get?\" },\n  { key: \"bonus_stack\", label: \"Bonus Stack\", question: \"What else do they get that makes the core feel undervalued?\" },\n  { key: \"guarantee\", label: \"Guarantee\", question: \"What happens if it doesn't work?\" },\n  { key: \"scarcity\", label: \"Scarcity / Urgency\", question: \"Why now, not later?\" },\n  { key: \"offer_name\", label: \"Name\", question: \"What is this thing called?\" },\n  { key: \"price\", label: \"Price + Payment\", question: \"What do they pay and how?\" },\n];\n\nexport function buildOfferAnatomy(offer: Offer): OfferAnatomyResult {\n  const components = ANATOMY_COMPONENTS.map((c) => {\n    const val = offer[c.key];\n    const present = val != null && val.trim().length > 0;\n    return { component: c.label, question: c.question, present, value: val };\n  });\n\n  const missing = components.filter((c) => !c.present).map((c) => c.component);\n  const complete = missing.length === 0;\n  const summary = complete\n    ? \"All 6 anatomy components present.\"\n    : `Missing: ${missing.join(\", \")}. Most weak offers fail on bonuses, guarantees, or scarcity.`;\n\n  return { complete, components, missing, summary };\n}\n\nexport interface OfferDiagnosis {\n  offer: Offer;\n  anatomy: OfferAnatomyResult;\n  valueEquation: ValueEquationResult;\n  priority: string;\n}\n\nexport function diagnoseOffer(offerId: string): OfferDiagnosis | null {\n  const offer = getOffer(offerId);\n  if (!offer) return null;\n\n  const anatomy = buildOfferAnatomy(offer);\n  const latestScore = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM value_equation_scores WHERE offer_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1`).get(offerId) as ValueEquationScore | undefined;\n  const valueEquation = latestScore\n    ? scoreValueEquation({\n        dream_outcome: latestScore.dream_outcome,\n        perceived_likelihood: latestScore.perceived_likelihood,\n        time_delay: latestScore.time_delay,\n        effort_sacrifice: latestScore.effort_sacrifice,\n      })\n    : scoreValueEquation({ dream_outcome: 5, perceived_likelihood: 5, time_delay: 5, effort_sacrifice: 5 });\n\n  const priority = anatomy.missing.length >= 3\n    ? `Fix anatomy first — missing ${anatomy.missing.length} components.`\n    : valueEquation.value < 25\n      ? `Value equation is weak (${valueEquation.value}/100). Fix the binding constraint: ${valueEquation.bindingConstraint}.`\n      : anatomy.complete\n        ? \"Offer is complete. Iterate on one lever at a time.\"\n        : `Offer is functional but incomplete: ${anatomy.missing.join(\", \")}.`;\n\n  return { offer, anatomy, valueEquation, priority };\n}\n\n// ── CRUD ──\n\nexport function listOffers(): Offer[] {\n  return db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM offers ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all() as Offer[];\n}\n\nexport function getOffer(id: string): Offer | undefined {\n  return db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM offers WHERE id = ?`).get(id) as Offer | undefined;\n}\n\nexport function createOffer(data: {\n  workspace_id?: string;\n  name: string;\n  business_type?: BusinessType;\n  core_deliverable?: string;\n  bonus_stack?: string;\n  guarantee?: string;\n  scarcity?: string;\n  offer_name?: string;\n  price?: string;\n  payment_structure?: string;\n  notes?: string;\n}): Offer {\n  const id = uid(\"off_\");\n  const ts = now();\n  db.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO offers (id, workspace_id, name, business_type, core_deliverable, bonus_stack, guarantee, scarcity, offer_name, price, payment_structure, status, notes, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'draft', ?, ?, ?)`,\n  ).run(id, data.workspace_id || \"default\", data.name, data.business_type || \"service\", data.core_deliverable || null, data.bonus_stack || null, data.guarantee || null, data.scarcity || null, data.offer_name || null, data.price || null, data.payment_structure || null, data.notes || null, ts, ts);\n  return getOffer(id)!;\n}\n\nexport function updateOffer(id: string, data: Partial<Omit<Offer, \"id\" | \"created_at\" | \"updated_at\">>): Offer | undefined {\n  const existing = getOffer(id);\n  if (!existing) return undefined;\n  const fields = Object.keys(data).filter((k) => k !== \"id\" && k !== \"created_at\" && k !== \"updated_at\");\n  if (fields.length === 0) return existing;\n  const setClause = fields.map((f) => `${f} = ?`).join(\", \");\n  const values = fields.map((f) => (data as Record<string, unknown>)[f]);\n  db.prepare(`UPDATE offers SET ${setClause}, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`).run(...values, now(), id);\n  return getOffer(id);\n}\n\nexport function deleteOffer(id: string): boolean {\n  return db.prepare(`DELETE FROM offers WHERE id = ?`).run(id).changes > 0;\n}\n\nexport function saveValueEquation(offerId: string, input: ValueEquationInput, notes?: string): ValueEquationResult {\n  const result = scoreValueEquation(input);\n  const id = uid(\"veq_\");\n  db.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO value_equation_scores (id, offer_id, dream_outcome, perceived_likelihood, time_delay, effort_sacrifice, computed_value, binding_constraint, notes, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,\n  ).run(id, offerId, input.dream_outcome, input.perceived_likelihood, input.time_delay, input.effort_sacrifice, result.value, result.bindingConstraint, notes || null, now());\n  return result;\n}\n\nexport function getValueEquationHistory(offerId: string): ValueEquationScore[] {\n  return db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM value_equation_scores WHERE offer_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC`).all(offerId) as ValueEquationScore[];\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/offers.ts"
}
```

```input
{
  "content": "import { NextRequest } from \"next/server\";\nimport { json, err, readJson } from \"@/lib/api-helpers\";\nimport { listOffers, getOffer, createOffer, updateOffer, deleteOffer, diagnoseOffer, saveValueEquation, type BusinessType } from \"@/lib/offers\";\n\nexport const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\n\nexport async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n  const id = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"id\");\n  const action = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"action\");\n\n  if (id && action === \"diagnose\") {\n    const diag = diagnoseOffer(id);\n    if (!diag) return err(\"Offer not found\", 404);\n    return json(diag);\n  }\n  if (id) {\n    const offer = getOffer(id);\n    if (!offer) return err(\"Offer not found\", 404);\n    return json(offer);\n  }\n  return json(listOffers());\n}\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  const body = await readJson<Record<string, unknown>>(req);\n  if (!body) return err(\"Invalid body\", 400);\n\n  if (body.action === \"create\") {\n    if (!body.name) return err(\"name required\", 400);\n    const offer = createOffer({\n      name: body.name as string,\n      business_type: body.business_type as BusinessType,\n      core_deliverable: body.core_deliverable as string,\n      bonus_stack: body.bonus_stack as string,\n      guarantee: body.guarantee as string,\n      scarcity: body.scarcityity as string,\n      offer_name: body.offer_name as string,\n      price: body.price as string,\n      payment_structure: body.payment_structure as string,\n      notes: body.notes as string,\n    });\n    return json(offer, 201);\n  }\n\n  if (body.action === \"update\") {\n    if (!body.id) return err(\"id required\", 400);\n    const offer = updateOffer(body.id as string, {\n      name: body.name as string,\n      business_type: body.business_type as BusinessType,\n      core_deliverable: body.core_deliverable as string,\n      bonus_stack: body.bonus_stack as string,\n      guarantee: body.guarantee as string,\n      scarcity: body.scarcity as string,\n      offer_name: body.offer_name as string,\n      price: body.price as string,\n      payment_structure: body.payment_structure as string,\n      status: body.status as string,\n      notes: body.notes as string,\n    });\n    if (!offer) return err(\"Offer not found\", 404);\n    return json(offer);\n  }\n\n  if (body.action === \"score\") {\n    if (!body.offer_id) return err(\"offer_id required\", 400);\n    const result = saveValueEquation(body.offer_id as string, {\n      dream_outcome: body.dream_outcome as number,\n      perceived_likelihood: body.perceived_likelihood as number,\n      time_delay: body.time_delay as number,\n      effort_sacrifice: body.effort_sacrifice as number,\n    }, body.notes as string);\n    return json(result);\n  }\n\n  return err(\"Unknown action. Use: create, update, score\", 400);\n}\n\nexport async function DELETE(req: NextRequest) {\n  const id = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"id\");\n  if (!id) return err(\"id required\", 400);\n  if (!deleteOffer(id)) return err(\"Offer not found\", 404);\n  return json({ ok: true });\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/app/api/offers/route.ts"
}
```

```input
{
  "content": "\"use client\";\nimport { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\nimport { Plus, Trash2, Sparkles, ChevronDown, ChevronRight, Target } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport { useFetch, apiFetch } from \"@/components/useFetch\";\n\ninterface Offer {\n  id: string;\n  name: string;\n  business_type: string;\n  core_deliverable: string | null;\n  bonus_stack: string | null;\n  guarantee: string | null;\n  scarcity: string | null;\n  offer_name: string | null;\n  price: string | null;\n  payment_structure: string | null;\n  status: string;\n  notes: string | null;\n  created_at: number;\n  updated_at: number;\n}\n\ninterface ValueEquation {\n  dream_outcome: number;\n  perceived_likelihood: number;\n  time_delay: number;\n  effort_sacrifice: number;\n}\n\nconst BUSINESS_TYPES = [\"service\", \"course\", \"coaching\", \"info\", \"saas\", \"agency\", \"b2b\"];\nconst ANATOMY_FIELDS: Array<{ key: string; label: string; placeholder: string }> = [\n  { key: \"core_deliverable\", label: \"Core Deliverable\", placeholder: \"What do they get?\" },\n  { key: \"bonus_stack\", label: \"Bonus Stack\", placeholder: \"What else do they get that makes the core feel undervalued?\" },\n  { key: \"guarantee\", label: \"Guarantee\", placeholder: \"What happens if it doesn't work?\" },\n  { key: \"scarcity\", label: \"Scarcity / Urgency\", placeholder: \"Why now, not later?\" },\n  { key: \"offer_name\", label: \"Offer Name\", placeholder: \"What is this thing called?\" },\n  { key: \"price\", label: \"Price + Payment\", placeholder: \"What do they pay and how?\" },\n];\n\nexport default function OffersPage() {\n  const { data, reload } = useFetch<Offer[]>(\"/api/offers\");\n  const [showForm, setShowForm] = useState(false);\n  const [expandedId, setExpandedId] = useState<string | null>(null);\n  const [diagnosis, setDiagnosis] = useState<Record<string, unknown> | null>(null);\n  const [diagLoading, setDiagLoading] = useState(false);\n\n  const offers = data || [];\n\n  async function runDiagnose(id: string) {\n    setDiagLoading(true);\n    try {\n      const r = await apiFetch(`/api/offers?id=${id}&action=diagnose`);\n      setDiagnosis(r);\n      setExpandedId(id);\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      alert(e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\");\n    }\n    setDiagLoading(false);\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"space-y-5\">\n      <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between\">\n        <div>\n          <h2 className=\"text-xl font-bold\">Offers</h2>\n          <p className=\"text-sm muted mt-0.5\">Build offers that move. Based on the $100M Offers value equation framework.</p>\n        </div>\n        <button className=\"btn btn-primary\" onClick={() => setShowForm(!showForm)}>\n          <Plus className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> New offer\n        </button>\n      </div>\n\n      {showForm && <OfferForm onDone={() => { setShowForm(false); reload(); }} />}\n\n      {offers.length === 0 ? (\n        <div className=\"card card-pad muted text-center py-12\">\n          <Target className=\"w-8 h-8 mx-auto mb-2 opacity-40\" />\n          No offers yet. Create one to start scoring its value equation.\n        </div>\n      ) : (\n        <div className=\"space-y-3\">\n          {offers.map((offer) => (\n            <div key={offer.id} className=\"card card-pad card-lift\">\n              <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between\">\n                <div className=\"flex items-center gap-3\">\n                  <button onClick={() => setExpandedId(expandedId === offer.id ? null : offer.id)} className=\"muted hover:text-text\">\n                    {expandedId === offer.id ? <ChevronDown className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> : <ChevronRight className=\"w-4 h-4\" />}\n                  </button>\n                  <div>\n                    <div className=\"font-semibold\">{offer.name}</div>\n                    <div className=\"text-xs muted\">\n                      <span className=\"capitalize\">{offer.business_type}</span>\n                      {offer.price && <span> · {offer.price}</span>}\n                      {offer.status === \"draft\" && <span className=\"ml-2 text-amber-600\">draft</span>}\n                    </div>\n                  </div>\n                </div>\n                <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2\">\n                  <button onClick={() => runDiagnose(offer.id)} disabled={diagLoading} className=\"btn btn-ghost text-sm\">\n                    <Sparkles className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> Diagnose\n                  </button>\n                  <DeleteButton id={offer.id} onDone={reload} />\n                </div>\n              </div>\n\n              {expandedId === offer.id && (\n                <div className=\"mt-4 pt-4 border-t border-border space-y-4\">\n                  <OfferDetail offer={offer} onUpdated={reload} />\n                  {diagnosis && expandedId === offer.id && <DiagnosisPanel diag={diagnosis} />}\n                </div>\n              )}\n            </div>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      )}\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction DeleteButton({ id, onDone }: { id: string; onDone: () => void }) {\n  async function del() {\n    if (!confirm(\"Delete this offer?\")) return;\n    await apiFetch(`/api/offers?id=${encodeURIComponent(id)}`, { method: \"DELETE\" });\n    onDone();\n  }\n  return <button onClick={del} className=\"text-red-500 hover:text-red-700\"><Trash2 className=\"w-4 h-4\" /></button>;\n}\n\nfunction OfferForm({ onDone }: { onDone: () => void }) {\n  const [name, setName] = useState(\"\");\n  const [businessType, setBusinessType] = useState(\"service\");\n  const [fields, setFields] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\n\n  async function submit(e: React.FormEvent) {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    if (!name.trim()) return;\n    setBusy(true);\n    try {\n      await apiFetch(\"/api/offers\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        body: JSON.stringify({ action: \"create\", name, business_type: businessType, ...fields }),\n      });\n      setName(\"\"); setFields({});\n      onDone();\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      alert(e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\");\n    }\n    setBusy(false);\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <form onSubmit={submit} className=\"card card-pad space-y-4\">\n      <div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-3\">\n        <div>\n          <label className=\"label\">Offer name</label>\n          <input className=\"input\" value={name} onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)} placeholder=\"e.g. Q4 Growth Sprint\" required />\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <label className=\"label\">Business type</label>\n          <select className=\"input\" value={businessType} onChange={(e) => setBusinessType(e.target.value)}>\n            {BUSINESS_TYPES.map((t) => <option key={t} value={t}>{t}</option>)}\n          </select>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n      {ANATOMY_FIELDS.map((f) => (\n        <div key={f.key}>\n          <label className=\"label\">{f.label}</label>\n          <textarea className=\"textarea\" rows={2} placeholder={f.placeholder} value={fields[f.key] || \"\"}\n            onChange={(e) => setFields({ ...fields, [f.key]: e.target.value })} />\n        </div>\n      ))}\n      <button type=\"submit\" disabled={busy} className=\"btn btn-primary\">{busy ? \"Saving…\" : \"Create offer\"}</button>\n    </form>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction OfferDetail({ offer, onUpdated }: { offer: Offer; onUpdated: () => void }) {\n  const [fields, setFields] = useState<Record<string, string>>({\n    core_deliverable: offer.core_deliverable || \"\",\n    bonus_stack: offer.bonus_stack || \"\",\n    guarantee: offer.guarantee || \"\",\n    scarcity: offer.scarcity || \"\",\n    offer_name: offer.offer_name || \"\",\n    price: offer.price || \"\",\n  });\n  const [ve, setVe] = useState<ValueEquation>({ dream_outcome: 5, perceived_likelihood: 5, time_delay: 5, effort_sacrifice: 5 });\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\n\n  async function saveFields() {\n    setBusy(true);\n    await apiFetch(\"/api/offers\", { method: \"POST\", body: JSON.stringify({ action: \"update\", id: offer.id, ...fields }) });\n    setBusy(false);\n    onUpdated();\n  }\n\n  async function scoreEquation() {\n    setBusy(true);\n    await apiFetch(\"/api/offers\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: JSON.stringify({ action: \"score\", offer_id: offer.id, ...ve }),\n    });\n    setBusy(false);\n    onUpdated();\n  }\n\n  const anatomyComplete = ANATOMY_FIELDS.every((f) => (fields[f.key] || \"\").trim().length > 0);\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"space-y-4\">\n      <div>\n        <h4 className=\"text-sm font-semibold mb-2\">Anatomy Checklist</h4>\n        <div className=\"space-y-2\">\n          {ANATOMY_FIELDS.map((f) => (\n            <div key={f.key}>\n              <label className=\"text-xs muted\">{f.label} {(fields[f.key] || \"\").trim() ? \"✓\" : \"—\"}</label>\n              <textarea className=\"textarea\" rows={1} placeholder={f.placeholder} value={fields[f.key] || \"\"}\n                onChange={(e) => setFields({ ...fields, [f.key]: e.target.value })} />\n            </div>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n        <button onClick={saveFields} disabled={busy} className=\"btn btn-ghost text-sm mt-2\">Save anatomy</button>\n        <span className={`ml-2 text-xs ${anatomyComplete ? \"text-emerald-600\" : \"text-amber-600\"}`}>\n          {anatomyComplete ? \"Complete\" : `${ANATOMY_FIELDS.filter((f) => !(fields[f.key] || \"\").trim()).length} missing`}\n        </span>\n      </div>\n\n      <ValueEquationWidget ve={ve} setVe={setVe} onScore={scoreEquation} busy={busy} />\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction ValueEquationWidget({ ve, setVe, onScore, busy }: { ve: ValueEquation; setVe: (v: ValueEquation) => void; onScore: () => void; busy: boolean }) {\n  const sliders: Array<{ key: keyof ValueEquation; label: string; direction: string }> = [\n    { key: \"dream_outcome\", label: \"Dream Outcome\", direction: \"↑ higher is better\" },\n    { key: \"perceived_likelihood\", label: \"Perceived Likelihood\", direction: \"↑ higher is better\" },\n    { key: \"time_delay\", label: \"Time Delay\", direction: \"↓ lower is better\" },\n    { key: \"effort_sacrifice\", label: \"Effort & Sacrifice\", direction: \"↓ lower is better\" },\n  ];\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"card card-pad bg-surface-2\">\n      <h4 className=\"text-sm font-semibold mb-3\">Value Equation</h4>\n      <div className=\"space-y-3\">\n        {sliders.map((s) => (\n          <div key={s.key}>\n            <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between\">\n              <label className=\"text-xs font-medium\">{s.label}</label>\n              <span className=\"text-xs muted\">{s.direction}</span>\n            </div>\n            <input type=\"range\" min={1} max={10} value={ve[s.key]}\n              onChange={(e) => setVe({ ...ve, [s.key]: parseInt(e.target.value) })}\n              className=\"w-full mt-1\" />\n            <div className=\"text-xs font-mono text-brand-500\">{ve[s.key]}/10</div>\n          </div>\n        ))}\n      </div>\n      <button onClick={onScore} disabled={busy} className=\"btn btn-primary text-sm mt-3\">\n        <Target className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> {busy ? \"Scoring…\" : \"Score & save\"}\n      </button>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction DiagnosisPanel({ diag }: { diag: Record<string, unknown> }) {\n  const ve = diag.valueEquation as { value: number; bindingConstraint: string; recommendation: string; leverScores: Array<{ lever: string; score: number; direction: string }> } | undefined;\n  const anatomy = diag.anatomy as { complete: boolean; missing: string[]; summary: string } | undefined;\n  const priority = diag.priority as string | undefined;\n\n  if (!ve || !anatomy) return null;\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"card card-pad border-l-4 border-brand-500 space-y-3\">\n      <div>\n        <h4 className=\"text-sm font-semibold flex items-center gap-2\"><Sparkles className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> Diagnosis</h4>\n        {priority && <p className=\"text-sm mt-1 font-medium\">{priority}</p>}\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-4\">\n        <div>\n          <div className=\"text-xs muted uppercase tracking-wide mb-1\">Value Score</div>\n          <div className=\"text-2xl font-bold\">{ve.value}<span className=\"text-sm muted\">/100</span></div>\n          <div className=\"text-xs muted mt-1\">Binding constraint: <span className=\"font-medium text-brand-500\">{ve.bindingConstraint}</span></div>\n          <p className=\"text-sm muted mt-2\">{ve.recommendation}</p>\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <div className=\"text-xs muted uppercase tracking-wide mb-1\">Anatomy</div>\n          {anatomy.complete ? (\n            <span className=\"text-sm text-emerald-600\">All 6 components present ✓</span>\n          ) : (\n            <div className=\"text-sm text-amber-600\">\n              Missing: {anatomy.missing.join(\", \")}\n            </div>\n          )}\n        </div>\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"flex flex-wrap gap-2 pt-1\">\n        {ve.leverScores.map((l) => (\n          <div key={l.lever} className=\"text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded-full bg-surface-2 border border-border\">\n            {l.lever}: {l.score}/10 {l.direction}\n          </div>\n        ))}\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}",

}
```

```output
/**
 * A provider is the concrete runtime unit. It owns id/name/base metadata,
 * auth methods, model listing, and stream behavior.
 *
 * `TApi` lets concrete provider factories declare which APIs their models
 * use (e.g. `openaiProvider(): Provider<"openai-responses" | "openai-completions">`),
 * giving typed model lists to direct factory users. Inside a `Models`
 * collection providers are held as `Provider<Api>`.
 */
export interface Provider<TApi extends Api = Api> {
    readonly id: string;
    readonly name: string;
    readonly baseUrl?: string;
    readonly headers?: ProviderHeaders;
    /**
     * Required: at least one of `apiKey`/`oauth`. Every provider has auth
     * semantics — even providers with only ambient credentials (env vars, AWS
     * profiles, ADC files) and keyless local servers provide `apiKey` auth
     * whose `resolve()` reports whether the provider is configured.
     * `Models.getAuth()` returns undefined when the provider is unconfigured.
     */
    readonly auth: ProviderAuth;
    /**
     * Current known models, sync. Static providers return their catalog;
     *
```

```output
 * Returns a stream synchronously while running async setup (auth resolution,
 * lazy module loading) behind it. Setup failures terminate the stream with an
 * error event.
 */

/**
 * Wraps a dynamically imported API implementation module as `ProviderStreams`.
 * The module loads on first stream call; the host's import cache deduplicates
 * loads. Load failures terminate the returned stream with an error event.
 */
```

```output
5:  * No external LLM required — pure deterministic reasoning. Can be upgraded to
6:  * call an LLM for free-form answers (set OPENAI_API_KEY) while keeping the same
7:  * structured data layer.
8:  *
9:  * Nova answers questions by:
10:  *   1. Classifying the question (intents: summary, comparison, recommendation, lookup, prediction)
11:  *   2. Pulling the relevant data from the DB
12:  *   3. Composing a structured answer with: answer, confidence, sources, suggested actions
13:  */
```

**tool `write`** completed
```input
{
  "content": "/**\n * Unified AI Provider — wraps @earendil-works/pi-ai to make the hub\n * provider-agnostic (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, xAI, DeepSeek, etc.).\n *\n * Replaces direct OpenAI fetch calls and the GLM-specific client with a\n * single entry point that auto-detects which provider is configured via\n * environment variables.\n *\n * Priority:\n *   1. OPENAI_API_KEY    → OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini default)\n *   2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY → Anthropic (claude-3-5-haiku default)\n *   3. GOOGLE_API_KEY    → Google (gemini-2.0-flash default)\n *   4. GROQ_API_KEY      → Groq (llama-3.3-70b default)\n *   5. XAI_API_KEY       → xAI (grok-2 default)\n *   6. DEEPSEEK_API_KEY  → DeepSeek (deepseek-chat default)\n *   7. GLM_API_KEY       → GLM/Zhipu (glm-4-plus, via OpenAI-compatible)\n *   8. No key            → returns null (caller uses offline fallback)\n */\n\nimport { builtinModels, type Model, type Context, type Message, type AssistantMessageEventStream } from \"@earendil-works/pi-ai\";\n\nexport interface AiChatMessage {\n  role: \"system\" | \"user\" | \"assistant\";\n  content: string;\n}\n\nexport interface AiChatResult {\n  content: string;\n  model: string;\n  provider: string;\n  tokens: number;\n  ok: boolean;\n  error?: string;\n}\n\nexport interface AiChatOptions {\n  model?: string;\n  temperature?: number;\n  maxTokens?: number;\n  signal?: AbortSignal;\n}\n\ninterface ProviderConfig {\n  name: string;\n  envKey: string;\n  defaultModel: string;\n  modelsId: string;\n}\n\nconst PROVIDERS: ProviderConfig[] = [\n  { name: \"openai\", envKey: \"OPENAI_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"gpt-4o-mini\", modelsId: \"openai\" },\n  { name: \"anthropic\", envKey: \"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"claude-3-5-haiku-latest\", modelsId: \"anthropic\" },\n  { name: \"google\", envKey: \"GOOGLE_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"gemini-2.0-flash\", modelsId: \"google\" },\n  { name: \"groq\", envKey: \"GROQ_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"llama-3.3-70b-versatile\", modelsId: \"groq\" },\n  { name: \"xai\", envKey: \"XAI_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"grok-2\", modelsId: \"xai\" },\n  { name: \"deepseek\", envKey: \"DEEPSEEK_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"deepseek-chat\", modelsId: \"deepseek\" },\n];\n\nfunction detectProvider(): ProviderConfig | null {\n  for (const p of PROVIDERS) {\n    const key = process.env[p.envKey];\n    if (key && !key.includes(\"REPLACE\") && key.length > 10) return p;\n  }\n  const glmKey = process.env.GLM_API_KEY || process.env.ZHIPU_API_KEY;\n  if (glmKey && !glmKey.includes(\"REPLACE\") && glmKey.length > 10) {\n    return { name: \"glm\", envKey: \"GLM_API_KEY\", defaultModel: \"glm-4-plus\", modelsId: \"glm\" };\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\nexport function isAiAvailable(): boolean {\n  return detectProvider() !== null;\n}\n\nexport function getProviderName(): string | null {\n  const p = detectProvider();\n  return p?.name ?? null;\n}\n\nexport function getDefaultModel(): string | null {\n  const p = detectProvider();\n  return p ? (process.env.AI_MODEL_OVERRIDE || p.defaultModel) : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Unified chat completion. Works with any configured provider.\n * Returns null if no provider is configured (caller should use offline fallback).\n */\nexport async function aiChat(\n  messages: AiChatMessage[],\n  opts: AiChatOptions = {},\n): Promise<AiChatResult> {\n  const provider = detectProvider();\n  if (!provider) {\n    return { content: \"\", model: \"none\", provider: \"none\", tokens: 0, ok: false, error: \"No AI provider configured\" };\n  }\n\n  const modelName = opts.model || process.env.AI_MODEL_OVERRIDE || provider.defaultModel;\n\n  // GLM uses OpenAI-compatible API — use direct fetch (pi-ai doesn't have a GLM provider)\n  if (provider.name === \"glm\") {\n    return glmCompatibleChat(messages, opts, modelName);\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const models = builtinModels();\n    const model = findModel(models, provider.modelsId, modelName);\n    if (!model) {\n      return glmCompatibleChat(messages, opts, modelName);\n    }\n\n    const context: Context = {\n      systemPrompt: messages.find((m) => m.role === \"system\")?.content,\n      messages: messages\n        .filter((m) => m.role !== \"system\")\n        .map((m): Message => ({\n          role: m.role === \"assistant\" ? \"assistant\" : \"user\",\n          content: m.content,\n          timestamp: Date.now(),\n        } as Message)),\n    };\n\n    const stream = models.streamSimple(model, context, {\n      temperature: opts.temperature ?? 0.6,\n      maxTokens: opts.maxTokens ?? 2000,\n      signal: opts.signal,\n      apiKey: process.env[provider.envKey] || undefined,\n    });\n\n    let content = \"\";\n    let tokens = 0;\n    for await (const event of stream) {\n      if (event.type === \"text\") {\n        content += event.text;\n      } else if (event.type === \"usage\" && event.usage) {\n        tokens = event.usage.inputTokens + event.usage.outputTokens;\n      } else if (event.type === \"error\") {\n        return { content, model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens, ok: false, error: event.error?.message || \"stream error\" };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { content: content.trim(), model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens, ok: true };\n  } catch (e: unknown) {\n    const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);\n    return { content: \"\", model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens: 0, ok: false, error: msg };\n  }\n}\n\nfunction findModel(models: ReturnType<typeof builtinModels>, providerId: string, modelId: string): Model | null {\n  const all = models.getModels();\n  for (const m of all) {\n    if (m.provider === providerId && (m.id === modelId || m.name === modelId)) return m;\n  }\n  for (const m of all) {\n    if (m.provider === providerId) return m;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/** Fallback: direct OpenAI-compatible chat for GLM or when pi-ai model lookup fails. */\nasync function glmCompatibleChat(\n  messages: AiChatMessage[],\n  opts: AiChatOptions,\n  modelName: string,\n): Promise<AiChatResult> {\n  const provider = detectProvider()!;\n  const baseUrl = provider.name === \"glm\"\n    ? \"https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4\"\n    : \"https://api.openai.com/v1\";\n  const apiKey = process.env[provider.envKey] || process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY || \"\";\n\n  try {\n    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n      body: JSON.stringify({\n        model: modelName,\n        messages: messages.map((m) => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })),\n        temperature: opts.temperature ?? 0.6,\n        max_tokens: opts.maxTokens ?? 2000,\n      }),\n      signal: opts.signal,\n    });\n    const data = await res.json() as Record<string, unknown>;\n    const choices = data.choices as Array<{ message: { content: string } }> | undefined;\n    const content = choices?.[0]?.message?.content?.trim() || \"\";\n    const usage = data.usage as { total_tokens: number } | undefined;\n    return { content, model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens: usage?.total_tokens || 0, ok: res.ok };\n  } catch (e: unknown) {\n    const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);\n    return { content: \"\", model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens: 0, ok: false, error: msg };\n  }\n}\n\nexport { AssistantMessageEventStream };",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/ai-provider.ts"
}
```

```input
  "newString": "/** AI audit summary using the unified provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/GLM/etc.) with offline fallback. */\nexport async function generateAuditSummary(result: SeoAuditResult): Promise<string> {\n  const { aiChat, isAiAvailable } = await import(\"@/lib/ai-provider\");\n  if (!isAiAvailable()) return offlineAuditSummary(result);\n\n  try {\n    const prompt = `You are an SEO expert. Summarize this audit in 3 sentences (state, root cause, priority action):\\n` +\n      `Score: ${result.score}/100\\n` +\n      `Errors: ${result.issues.filter((i) => i.level === \"error\").map((i) => i.message).join(\", \") || \"none\"}\\n` +\n      `Warnings: ${result.issues.filter((i) => i.level === \"warning\").map((i) => i.message).join(\", \") || \"none\"}\\n` +\n      `Word count: ${result.metrics.wordCount}, Internal links: ${result.metrics.internalLinks}, Schema: ${result.metrics.hasSchema}`;\n    const r = await aiChat(\n      [{ role: \"system\", content: \"You are an SEO expert. Respond in 3 sentences.\" }, { role: \"user\", content: prompt }],\n      { temperature: 0.3, maxTokens: 200 },\n    );\n    if (r.ok && r.content) return r.content;\n    return offlineAuditSummary(result);\n  } catch {\n    return offlineAuditSummary(result);\n  }\n}",
  "oldString": "/** AI audit summary using OpenAI if configured, otherwise offline fallback. */\nexport async function generateAuditSummary(result: SeoAuditResult): Promise<string> {\n  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;\n  if (!apiKey) return offlineAuditSummary(result);\n\n  try {\n    const prompt = `You are an SEO expert. Summarize this audit in 3 sentences (state, root cause, priority action):\\n` +\n      `Score: ${result.score}/100\\n` +\n      `Errors: ${result.issues.filter((i) => i.level === \"error\").map((i) => i.message).join(\", \") || \"none\"}\\n` +\n      `Warnings: ${result.issues.filter((i) => i.level === \"warning\").map((i) => i.message).join(\", \") || \"none\"}\\n` +\n      `Word count: ${result.metrics.wordCount}, Internal links: ${result.metrics.internalLinks}, Schema: ${result.metrics.hasSchema}`;\n    const res = await fetch(\"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`, \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ model: \"gpt-4o-mini\", messages: [{ role: \"user\", content: prompt }], max_tokens: 200, temperature: 0.3 }),\n    });\n    const data = await res.json() as Record<string, unknown>;\n    const text = (data.choices as Array<{ message: { content: string } }>)?.[0]?.message?.content;\n    return text?.trim() || offlineAuditSummary(result);\n  } catch {\n    return offlineAuditSummary(result);\n  }\n}"
```

```input
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/glm.ts",
  "newString": "export async function glmChat(\n  messages: GLMMessage[],\n  options?: { model?: string; temperature?: number; maxTokens?: number },\n): Promise<GLMResponse> {\n  const client = getGLMClient();\n\n  // If GLM is configured, use it directly (fastest path for existing setups)\n  if (client) {\n    try {\n      const model = options?.model || GLM_MODELS.plus;\n      const completion = await client.chat.completions.create({\n        model,\n        messages: messages as unknown[],\n        temperature: options?.temperature ?? 0.7,\n        max_tokens: options?.maxTokens ?? 4096,\n      });\n      const content = completion.choices[0]?.message?.content || \"\";\n      return {\n        content,\n        model,\n        tokens: completion.usage?.total_tokens || 0,\n        ok: true,\n      };\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      return { content: \"\", model: \"glm-4\", tokens: 0, ok: false, error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"GLM error\" };\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fallback: try the unified AI provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Groq/xAI/DeepSeek)\n  try {\n    const { aiChat } = require(\"@/lib/ai-provider\");\n    const r = await aiChat(messages, {\n      model: options?.model,\n      temperature: options?.temperature,\n      maxTokens: options?.maxTokens,\n    });\n    return {\n      content: r.content,\n      model: r.model,\n      tokens: r.tokens,\n      ok: r.ok,\n      error: r.error,\n    };\n  } catch {\n    return {\n      content: \"\",\n      model: \"none\",\n      tokens: 0,\n      ok: false,\n      error: \"No AI provider configured. Set OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, or GLM_API_KEY.\",\n    };\n  }\n}",
```

Now I know the event types. Let me fix the streaming logic in ai-provider.ts:
- `text_delta` has `delta` (not `text`)
- `text_end` has `content` (the full text)
- `done` has `message` with `usage`
- `error` has `error` with `errorMessage`

**tool `edit`** completed
```input
{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/ai-provider.ts",
  "newString": "    const stream = models.streamSimple(model, context, {\n      temperature: opts.temperature ?? 0.6,\n      maxTokens: opts.maxTokens ?? 2000,\n      signal: opts.signal,\n      apiKey: process.env[provider.envKey] || undefined,\n    });\n\n    let content = \"\";\n    let tokens = 0;\n    for await (const event of stream) {\n      if (event.type === \"text_delta\") {\n        content += event.delta;\n      } else if (event.type === \"text_end\") {\n        content = event.content;\n      } else if (event.type === \"done\" && event.message?.usage) {\n        tokens = (event.message.usage.inputTokens || 0) + (event.message.usage.outputTokens || 0);\n      } else if (event.type === \"error\") {\n        return { content, model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens, ok: false, error: event.error?.errorMessage || \"stream error\" };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { content: content.trim(), model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens, ok: true };",
  "oldString": "    const stream = models.streamSimple(model, context, {\n      temperature: opts.temperature ?? 0.6,\n      maxTokens: opts.maxTokens ?? 2000,\n      signal: opts.signal,\n      apiKey: process.env[provider.envKey] || undefined,\n    });\n\n    let content = \"\";\n    let tokens = 0;\n    for await (const event of stream) {\n      if (event.type === \"text\") {\n        content += event.text;\n      } else if (event.type === \"usage\" && event.usage) {\n        tokens = event.usage.inputTokens + event.usage.outputTokens;\n      } else if (event.type === \"error\") {\n        return { content, model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens, ok: false, error: event.error?.message || \"stream error\" };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { content: content.trim(), model: modelName, provider: provider.name, tokens, ok: true };"
```

### [assistant] 2026-07-14T10:08:43.935000+00:00  @glm-5.2


Done. Here's what the Pi upgrade delivered:

**Unified AI Provider** (`src/lib/ai-provider.ts`):
- Auto-detects which provider is configured via env vars (priority: OpenAI → Anthropic → Google → Groq → xAI → DeepSeek → GLM)
- Single `aiChat()` function replaces all direct OpenAI/GLM fetch calls
- Uses pi-ai's `builtinModels()` + `streamSimple()` for native streaming
- Falls back to OpenAI-compatible fetch for GLM (which pi-ai doesn't have a native provider for)
- `isAiAvailable()`, `getProviderName()`, `getDefaultModel()` for status checks
- New `/api/ai/status` endpoint for the settings UI

**Updated call sites**:
- `seo.ts` — AI audit summaries now use the unified provider instead of direct OpenAI fetch
- `glm.ts` — `isGLMAvailable()` and `glmChat()` now fall through to the unified provider if GLM isn't configured, so the entire hub (machine, agents, quality-gate, marketing-engine, ideas, strategist) automatically gains multi-provider support without touching their code

**Env vars** (any one activates the hub's AI features):
`OPENAI_API_KEY` · `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` · `GOOGLE_API_KEY` · `GROQ_API_KEY` · `XAI_API_KEY` · `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` · `GLM_API_KEY`

Optional: `AI_MODEL_OVERRIDE` to force a specific model across all providers.

```output
---
// Tiny client-side data hook to keep page code DRY.
"use client";
import { useEffect, useCallback, useRef, useState } from "react";
import { useToast } from "./Toast";

export function useFetch<T = Record<string, unknown>>(
  url: string | null,
  opts?: { silent?: boolean },
) {
  const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const toast = useToast();
  const silent = opts?.silent ?? false;
  const firstLoad = useRef(true);

  const reload = useCallback(async () => {
    if (!url) return;
    setLoading(true);
    setError(null);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(url, { cache: "no-store" });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
      const json: T = await res.json();
      setData(json);
    } catch (e) {
      const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
      setError(msg);
      if (!silent && !firstLoad.current) {
        toast.error("Couldn't load data", msg);
      }
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
      firstLoad.current = false;
    }
  }, [url, silent, toast]);

  useEffect(() => {
    reload();
  }, [reload]);

  return { data, loading, error, reload, setData };
}

export async function apiFetch(url: string, opts: RequestInit = {}): Promise<unknown> {
  const res = await fetch(url, {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...(opts.headers || {}) },
    ...opts,
  });
  if (!res.ok) {
  
... [+228 chars truncated]
```

```output
# Heuristics & Similar-Knowledge Audit — Marketing Hub

> **Status: IMPLEMENTED.** All 16 entries below have been replaced with real
> models, calibrated scoring, or doctrine-labeled checklists. The shared
> logistic-regression module lives at `src/lib/ml/logistic.ts`. Each entry's
> "Replace" section now describes what was shipped, not what to do.
>
> **Goal: leave no heuristic undocumented.** Every hand-tuned rule, magic
> number, regex-threshold, fixed weight, and doctrinal formula in the hub is
> listed here with: the actual rule, the data it rests on, how to measure it,
> how to tell it apart from a real model, and the concrete replacement ready to
> implement.

## Legend

- **HEURISTIC** — a rule with weights/thresholds chosen by a human, never
  re-fit, same for every tenant. Replace with a fitted model.
- **DOCTRINE** — a cited framework from a named master or peer-reviewed paper
  (Cialdini, Schwartz, Kennedy, Porter, Lynn, Rogers). Keep, but expose
  calibration so users can see how it performs on their data.
- **FAKE-DATA** — code that synthesizes numbers instead of measuring them.
  Flag and remove or replace with real instrumentation.

## Operating rule (applies to every entry below)

A heuristic must ship with its **own measurement**: calibration curve, AUC,
Cohen's kappa, or statistical power — computed from real hub data and shown in
the UI next to the score. A heuristic you can't measure is a guess. A
heuristic you can measure either earns its place (beats ba
... [+2283 chars truncated]
```

```output
/**
 * Seed-surface framework — generic manual-entry surfaces backed by their own
 * SQLite table, created lazily via a self-healing migration.
 *
 * Each surface is identified by a slug (e.g. "podcast", "video") and has:
 *  - a dedicated table `seed_<slug>` (id, title, status, category, body, url,
 *    meta JSON, created_at, updated_at)
 *  - CRUD via listSeed/createSeed
 *  - a seed of realistic sample rows on first access
 *
 * This lets the 12 no-scrape-source surfaces ship now as manual-entry apps
 * with adapter hooks left for future ingest.
 */
import { db, now, uid } from "@/lib/db";

export interface SeedSurfaceConfig {
  slug: string;
  label: string;
  icon?: string;
  statuses: string[];
  categories: string[];
  seed: { title: string; status: string; category: string; body: string; url?: string; meta?: Record<string, unknown> }[];
}

export interface SeedRow {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  status: string;
  category: string;
  body: string;
  url: string;
  meta: Record<string, unknown>;
  created_at: number;
  updated_at: number;
}

const TABLE = (slug: string) => `seed_${slug.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi, "_").toLowerCase()}`;
const seeded = new Set<string>();

/** Lazily create the surface table + seed it once. */
export function ensureSeedSurface(cfg: SeedSurfaceConfig): void {
  const table = TABLE(cfg.slug);
  if (seeded.has(table)) return;
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${table} (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      status
... [+2327 chars truncated]
```

```output
import { NextRequest } from "next/server";
import { json } from "@/lib/api-helpers";
import { ensureSeedSurface, listSeed, createSeed } from "@/lib/seed-surface";
import { SEED_SURFACES } from "@/lib/seed-surfaces";

export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";

const BY_SLUG = new Map(SEED_SURFACES.map((s) => [s.slug, s]));

/** GET /api/seed/[slug] — list rows. POST — create a row. */
export async function GET(req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { slug: string } }) {
  try {
    const cfg = BY_SLUG.get(params.slug);
    if (!cfg) return json({ error: "Unknown surface" }, 404);
    ensureSeedSurface(cfg);
    const status = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get("status") || undefined;
    return json({ items: listSeed(cfg.slug, status), config: { statuses: cfg.statuses, categories: cfg.categories, label: cfg.label } });
  } catch (e) {
    return json({ error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : "Failed" }, 500);
  }
}

export async function POST(req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { slug: string } }) {
  try {
    const cfg = BY_SLUG.get(params.slug);
    if (!cfg) return json({ error: "Unknown surface" }, 404);
    ensureSeedSurface(cfg);
    const body = await req.json();
    if (!body.title) return json({ error: "title is required" }, 400);
    const id = createSeed(cfg.slug, {
      title: body.title,
      status: body.status,
      category: body.category,
      body: body.body,
      url: body.url,
      meta: body.meta,
    });
    return json({ id }, 201);
  } catch (e
... [+92 chars truncated]
```

```output
# Marketing Documentation

A curated set of marketing reference docs compiled from authoritative sources on
the web. Each file summarizes key concepts and best practices, then lists the
source URLs so you can go deeper.

> Note: Snippets were gathered via web search. Treat these docs as a starting
> reference; always verify platform-specific rules against the primary source,
> as they change frequently.

## Contents

| File | Topic |
| --- | --- |
| [01-digital-marketing-fundamentals.md](01-digital-marketing-fundamentals.md) | Core channels, media models, funnel, measurement |
| [02-seo.md](02-seo.md) | Search engine optimization (Google-first) |
| [03-email-marketing.md](03-email-marketing.md) | Deliverability, authentication, list health |
| [04-social-media-marketing.md](04-social-media-marketing.md) | Platform strategy, content, engagement |
| [05-paid-ads-ppc.md](05-paid-ads-ppc.md) | Google Ads structure & campaign best practices |
| [06-content-marketing.md](06-content-marketing.md) | Strategy framework, production, distribution |

## How the channels fit together

The dominant theme across every source: **integration**. No single channel wins
alone. A strong system looks like this:

- **Owned media** (site, blog, email list) — assets you control and that compound.
- **Earned media** (press, shares, reviews, word of mouth) — highest credibility.
- **Paid media** (search/social/display ads) — fast, con
```

```input
{
  "content": "# Plan: Biggest Marketing Services Integration\n\n## Goal\nTurn Marketing-for-Engineers (373 entries) + marketingskills (47 skills) into the largest actionable marketing services layer in the hub. Target: +1 unified services catalog + ~30 new operational surfaces.\n\n## Context (verified)\n- `marketing-hub` already has 80+ routes, seed-surface pattern (`lib/seed-surface.ts`), batch scaffolder (`scripts/add-feature.ts`), unified AI provider (`lib/ai-provider.ts`), and global search via `loot_index`.\n- Parsed MFE data lives at `/tmp/kilo/mfe-parsed.json`: 281 articles, 70 tools, 22 checklists across 15 categories.\n- 39 of 47 marketingskills are missing from the hub; 8 overlap with existing surfaces (ads, analytics, emails, offers, competitors, onboarding, pricing, referrals, sms, social, video — exact overlap count is 11, not 8; see below).\n- Latest commit (`60dda9c`) added heuristics audit + batch scripts. No MFE integration yet.\n\n## Decisions\n\n### 1. MFE 373 entries → unified catalog, not 373 pages\n**Rationale:** 373 individual routes would bloat the sidebar and duplicate existing surfaces. Instead, create `/services` as a searchable catalog backed by a single `marketing_services` table. Each entry gets an actionable \"Launch\" button:\n- **Tools** (70) → open in a modal or dedicated executor page (`/services/tool/[id]`).\n- **Checklists** (22) → guided workflow (`/services/checklist/[id]`).\n- **Articles** (281) → knowledge card with AI-generated summary + 3 action items.\n\n### 2. Data model\nNew table `marketing_services`:\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS marketing_services (\n  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  title TEXT NOT NULL,\n  url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n  category TEXT NOT NULL,\n  subcategory TEXT,\n  type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'article', -- article|tool|checklist\n  description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n  source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'mfe',\n  meta TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',\n  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,\n  updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL\n);\nCREATE INDEX idx_marketing_services_category ON marketing_services(category);\nCREATE INDEX idx_marketing_services_type ON marketing_services(type);\n```\n\n### 3. Batch-add missing marketingskills as individual surfaces\nUse `scripts/add-feature.ts --batch` to scaffold the ~30 truly new skills as seed surfaces. Each gets the standard pattern: `src/app/<slug>/page.tsx`, `src/lib/seed-surfaces.ts` entry, Sidebar nav item, and generic CRUD API via `/api/seed/<slug>`.\n\n**Exact missing list (30 new surfaces):**\n`ad-creative`, `ai-seo`, `aso`, `churn-prevention`, `cold-email`, `co-marketing`, `community-marketing`, `content-strategy`, `copy-editing`, `copywriting`, `cro`, `customer-research`, `directory-submissions`, `free-tools`, `image`, `launch`, `marketing-council`, `marketing-ideas`, `marketing-loops`, `marketing-plan`, `marketing-psychology`, `paywalls`, `popups`, `product-marketing`, `programmatic-seo`, `prospecting`, `public-relations`, `revops`, `sales-enablement`, `schema`, `seo-audit`, `signup`, `site-architecture`\n\n**Overlap (skip — already have surfaces):**\n`ab-testing` (→ experiments), `competitor-profiling` (→ competitors), `competitors` (→ competitors), `emails` (→ emails/emails), `lead-magnets` (→ lead-magnets), `onboarding` (→ onboarding), `pricing` (→ pricing), `referrals` (→ referral), `sms` (→ sms), `social` (→ social), `video` (→ video)\n\n### 4. Tool and checklist executability\n- Create `src/lib/services/tools.ts` — wrappers that map MFE tool titles to existing calculators or simple interactive forms.\n- Create `src/lib/services/checklists.ts` — state-machine for checklist progress (steps, completion, save to `marketing_service_runs` table).\n- `/services/tool/[id]` and `/services/checklist/[id]` pages use the unified AI provider for any LLM-dependent steps.\n\n### 5. AI action layer\nEvery article card in `/services` gets an \"AI Action Plan\" button that calls `/api/ai/status`-gated provider to generate 3 executable steps. Tool/checklist pages also get inline AI coaching.\n\n## Execution order\n\n### Phase 1 — MFE catalog (biggest impact, fastest)\n1. Add `marketing_services` table migration in `src/lib/db/index.ts`.\n2. Write `/tmp/kilo/import-mfe.py` to ingest `/tmp/kilo/mfe-parsed.json` into `marketing_services`.\n3. Create `/api/services` route (GET with `?category=&type=&q=` filters, facet counts).\n4. Create `/services` page with category pills, type tabs, search, and card grid.\n5. Create `/services/tool/[id]` and `/services/checklist/[id]` executor pages.\n6. Add Sidebar entry: `{ href: \"/services\", label: \"Services\", icon: Wrench }`.\n7. Run import script; verify `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM marketing_services` = 373.\n\n### Phase 2 — Batch skill surfaces\n1. Run `tsx scripts/add-feature.ts --batch` with a JSON containing the 30 missing skills.\n2. For each new surface, verify `/api/seed/<slug>` returns 200 and page renders.\n3. Re-run Sidebar generation/verification.\n\n### Phase 3 — Deep tool wrappers\n1. Map the 70 MFE tools to existing calculators or build 20–30 lightweight interactive wrappers (e.g., persona generator, headline evaluator, budget allocator).\n2. Wire wrappers into `/services/tool/[id]`.\n\n### Phase 4 — Checklist workflows\n1. Convert 22 checklists into step-by-step workflows with progress persistence in `marketing_service_runs` (id, service_id, workspace_id, steps JSON, completed_at).\n2. Wire into `/services/checklist/[id]`.\n\n## Validation\n- `pnpm typecheck` passes.\n- `/services` loads, filters, and shows 373 entries.\n- Every new seed surface returns 200.\n- AI action plan button returns a non-empty plan for any article.\n- No duplicate sidebar entries.\n\n## Risks\n- **Sidebar bloat**: adding 30 surfaces makes the nav very long. Mitigation: group them under \"Services\" collapsible section, or use a nested submenu pattern.\n- **Tool coverage gap**: 70 tools can't all become full interactive apps. Mitigation: tier them — Tier 1 (top 20) get custom wrappers, Tier 2 (next 30) get template forms, Tier 3 (rest) link out to original URL with hub tracking.\n- **Duplicate seed-surface names**: some skill slugs may collide with existing ones. Mitigation: the batch script checks `BY_SLUG` before writing; log and skip collisions.\n\n## Open question\nShould the 30 new skill surfaces be grouped under a new \"Services\" sidebar collapsible, or stay flat in their logical groups (e.g., `cro` under Revenue, `copywriting` under Creative)?\n\n**Recommended answer:** Flat placement in their existing logical groups keeps the sidebar familiar and avoids a mega-group. Use the batch script's `--group` flag to assign each skill to the correct department group.\n",
```

Now let me create the services library module.

**tool `write`** completed
```input
{
  "content": "/**\n * Marketing Services — unified catalog of actionable marketing tactics,\n * tools, and checklists. Fed by the Marketing-for-Engineers corpus and\n * extensible to other sources.\n */\nimport { db, now, uid } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nexport interface MarketingService {\n  id: string;\n  title: string;\n  url: string;\n  category: string;\n  subcategory: string | null;\n  type: \"article\" | \"tool\" | \"checklist\";\n  description: string;\n  source: string;\n  meta: Record<string, unknown>;\n  created_at: number;\n  updated_at: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ServiceRun {\n  id: string;\n  service_id: string;\n  workspace_id: string | null;\n  user_id: string | null;\n  steps: RunStep[];\n  status: string;\n  completed_at: number | null;\n  created_at: number;\n  updated_at: number;\n}\n\nexport interface RunStep {\n  label: string;\n  done: boolean;\n}\n\nlet _imported = false;\n\nexport interface ImportEntry {\n  title: string;\n  url?: string;\n  category: string;\n  subcategory?: string | null;\n  type?: string;\n  description?: string;\n  is_checklist?: boolean;\n  author?: string | null;\n}\n\nexport function importServices(entries: ImportEntry[], source = \"mfe\"): number {\n  let inserted = 0;\n  const t = now();\n  const stmt = db.prepare(\n    `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO marketing_services (id, title, url, category, subcategory, type, description, source, meta, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`,\n  );\n  for (const e of entries) {\n    const type: string = e.type || (e.is_checklist ? \"checklist\" : \"article\");\n    const id = uid(\"svc\");\n    const r = stmt.run(\n      id,\n      e.title,\n      e.url || \"\",\n      e.category,\n      e.subcategory || null,\n      type,\n      e.description || \"\",\n      source,\n      JSON.stringify({ author: e.author || null }),\n      t,\n      t,\n    );\n    if (r.changes > 0) inserted++;\n  }\n  _imported = true;\n  return inserted;\n}\n\nexport function ensureImported(entries: ImportEntry[], source = \"mfe\"): void {\n  if (_imported) return;\n  const count = (db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) c FROM marketing_services WHERE source = ?`).get(source) as { c: number }).c;\n  if (count === 0) importServices(entries, source);\n  _imported = true;\n}\n\nexport function listServices(opts?: {\n  category?: string;\n  type?: string;\n  q?: string;\n  limit?: number;\n}): { rows: MarketingService[]; facets: { categories: Record<string, number>; types: Record<string, number> }; total: number } {\n  const limit = Math.min(opts?.limit || 400, 1000);\n  const where: string[] = [];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n  if (opts?.category && opts.category !== \"all\") {\n    where.push(\"category = ?\");\n    params.push(opts.category);\n  }\n  if (opts?.type && opts.type !== \"all\") {\n    where.push(\"type = ?\");\n    params.push(opts.type);\n  }\n  if (opts?.q) {\n    where.push(\"(title LIKE ? OR description LIKE ?)\");\n    params.push(`%${opts.q}%`, `%${opts.q}%`);\n  }\n  const clause = where.length ? `WHERE ${where.join(\" AND \")}` : \"\";\n  const rows = db\n    .prepare(`SELECT * FROM marketing_services ${clause} ORDER BY category, title LIMIT ?`)\n    .all(...params, limit) as Record<string, unknown>[];\n\n  const totalRow = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) c FROM marketing_services ${clause}`).get(...params) as { c: number };\n  const catRows = db.prepare(`SELECT category, COUNT(*) c FROM marketing_services GROUP BY category ORDER BY c DESC`).all() as { category: string; c: number }[];\n  const typeRows = db.prepare(`SELECT type, COUNT(*) c FROM marketing_services GROUP BY type`).all() as { type: string; c: number }[];\n\n  return {\n    rows: rows.map(mapRow),\n    total: totalRow.c,\n    facets: {\n      categories: Object.fromEntries(catRows.map((r) => [r.category, r.c])),\n      types: Object.fromEntries(typeRows.map((r) => [r.type, r.c])),\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport function getService(id: string): MarketingService | null {\n  const row = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM marketing_services WHERE id = ?`).get(id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n  return row ? mapRow(row) : null;\n}\n\nexport function listRuns(serviceId: string, workspaceId?: string): ServiceRun[] {\n  const rows = workspaceId\n    ? db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM marketing_service_runs WHERE service_id = ? AND workspace_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all(serviceId, workspaceId)\n    : db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM marketing_service_runs WHERE service_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all(serviceId);\n  return (rows as Record<string, unknown>[]).map(mapRunRow);\n}\n\nexport function createRun(serviceId: string, steps: RunStep[], workspaceId?: string, userId?: string): string {\n  const id = uid(\"run\");\n  const t = now();\n  db.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO marketing_service_runs (id, service_id, workspace_id, user_id, steps, status, completed_at, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,'in_progress',NULL,?,?)`,\n  ).run(id, serviceId, workspaceId || null, userId || null, JSON.stringify(steps), t, t);\n  return id;\n}\n\nexport function updateRun(runId: string, steps: RunStep[]): void {\n  const t = now();\n  const allDone = steps.every((s) => s.done);\n  db.prepare(\n    `UPDATE marketing_service_runs SET steps = ?, status = ?, completed_at = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`,\n  ).run(JSON.stringify(steps), allDone ? \"completed\" : \"in_progress\", allDone ? t : null, t, runId);\n}\n\nfunction mapRow(r: Record<string, unknown>): MarketingService {\n  let meta: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n  try { meta = JSON.parse(String(r.meta || \"{}\")); } catch { /* ignore */ }\n  return {\n    id: String(r.id),\n    title: String(r.title),\n    url: String(r.url || \"\"),\n    category: String(r.category),\n    subcategory: r.subcategory ? String(r.subcategory) : null,\n    type: String(r.type || \"article\") as MarketingService[\"type\"],\n    description: String(r.description || \"\"),\n    source: String(r.source || \"mfe\"),\n    meta,\n    created_at: Number(r.created_at) || 0,\n    updated_at: Number(r.updated_at) || 0,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction mapRunRow(r: Record<string, unknown>): ServiceRun {\n  let steps: RunStep[] = [];\n  try { steps = JSON.parse(String(r.steps || \"[]\")); } catch { /* ignore */ }\n  return {\n    id: String(r.id),\n    service_id: String(r.service_id),\n    workspace_id: r.workspace_id ? String(r.workspace_id) : null,\n    user_id: r.user_id ? String(r.user_id) : null,\n    steps,\n    status: String(r.status || \"in_progress\"),\n    completed_at: r.completed_at ? Number(r.completed_at) : null,\n    created_at: Number(r.created_at) || 0,\n    updated_at: Number(r.updated_at) || 0,\n  };\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/services.ts"
}
```

Now the `/api/services/[id]/run` route for managing checklist runs.

**tool `write`** completed
```input
{
  "content": "import { NextRequest } from \"next/server\";\nimport { json } from \"@/lib/api-helpers\";\nimport { getService, listRuns, createRun, updateRun } from \"@/lib/services\";\nimport { aiChat } from \"@/lib/ai-provider\";\n\nexport const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\n\nexport async function GET(_req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {\n  try {\n    const svc = getService(params.id);\n    if (!svc) return json({ error: \"Service not found\" }, 404);\n    const runs = listRuns(params.id);\n    return json({ service: svc, runs });\n  } catch (e) {\n    return json({ error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\" }, 500);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {\n  try {\n    const svc = getService(params.id);\n    if (!svc) return json({ error: \"Service not found\" }, 404);\n    const body = await req.json();\n\n    // Action plan: generate 3 actionable steps from article content via AI\n    if (body.action === \"action-plan\") {\n      const result = await aiChat([\n        { role: \"system\", content: \"You are a marketing strategist. Generate exactly 3 concrete, actionable next steps based on the marketing resource below. Return ONLY a JSON array of strings, each a single action step. No prose.\" },\n        { role: \"user\", content: `Title: ${svc.title}\\nCategory: ${svc.category}\\nType: ${svc.type}\\nDescription: ${svc.description || \"(none)\"}\\nURL: ${svc.url || \"(none)\"}` },\n      ], { temperature: 0.4, maxTokens: 300 });\n\n      if (!result.ok) {\n        return json({ error: result.error || \"AI unavailable\", steps: [] }, 503);\n      }\n\n      let steps: string[] = [];\n      try {\n        const parsed = JSON.parse(result.content);\n        if (Array.isArray(parsed)) steps = parsed.map((s: unknown) => String(s)).slice(0, 3);\n      } catch {\n        steps = result.content.split(\"\\n\").map((s) => s.replace(/^\\d+\\.\\s*/, \"\").trim()).filter(Boolean).slice(0, 3);\n      }\n      if (steps.length === 0) steps = [\"Review the resource and identify one immediate action.\", \"Share findings with your team.\", \"Schedule a follow-up to measure impact.\"];\n      return json({ steps, model: result.model, provider: result.provider });\n    }\n\n    // Create a checklist run\n    if (body.action === \"start\" || body.steps) {\n      let steps = body.steps as { label: string; done: boolean }[] | undefined;\n      if (!steps && svc.type === \"checklist\") {\n        const gen = await aiChat([\n          { role: \"system\", content: \"You are a marketing checklist designer. Break the resource below into 5-8 actionable checklist steps. Return ONLY a JSON array of strings (step labels). No prose.\" },\n          { role: \"user\", content: `Title: ${svc.title}\\nCategory: ${svc.category}\\nDescription: ${svc.description || \"(none)\"}` },\n        ], { temperature: 0.3, maxTokens: 300 });\n\n        let labels: string[] = [];\n        if (gen.ok) {\n          try {\n            const parsed = JSON.parse(gen.content);\n            if (Array.isArray(parsed)) labels = parsed.map((s: unknown) => String(s));\n          } catch {\n            labels = gen.content.split(\"\\n\").map((s) => s.replace(/^\\d+\\.\\s*/, \"\").trim()).filter(Boolean);\n          }\n        }\n        if (labels.length === 0) labels = [\"Step 1\", \"Step 2\", \"Step 3\", \"Step 4\", \"Step 5\"];\n        steps = labels.map((label) => ({ label, done: false }));\n      }\n      if (!steps) steps = [{ label: \"Complete this task\", done: false }];\n      const runId = createRun(params.id, steps, body.workspace_id, body.user_id);\n      return json({ id: runId, steps }, 201);\n    }\n\n    return json({ error: \"Unknown action\" }, 400);\n  } catch (e) {\n    return json({ error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\" }, 500);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const runId = body.run_id as string;\n    const steps = body.steps as { label: string; done: boolean }[];\n    if (!runId || !steps) return json({ error: \"run_id and steps required\" }, 400);\n    updateRun(runId, steps);\n    return json({ ok: true });\n  } catch (e) {\n    return json({ error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\" }, 500);\n  }\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/app/api/services/[id]/run/route.ts"
}
```

```input
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env tsx\n/**\n * Import the parsed Marketing-for-Engineers corpus into the\n * marketing_services table. Idempotent — safe to re-run.\n *\n * Usage: tsx scripts/import-mfe.ts\n */\nimport { readFileSync } from \"node:fs\";\nimport { join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { db } from \"../src/lib/db\";\nimport { importServices } from \"../src/lib/services\";\n\nconst SOURCE = join(process.cwd(), \"..\", \"..\", \"tmp\", \"kilo\", \"mfe-parsed.json\");\n\ntry {\n  const raw = readFileSync(SOURCE, \"utf-8\");\n  const entries = JSON.parse(raw);\n  if (!Array.isArray(entries)) throw new Error(\"Expected array of entries\");\n  const inserted = importServices(entries, \"mfe\");\n  const count = (db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) c FROM marketing_services WHERE source = 'mfe'`).get() as { c: number }).c;\n  console.log(`Imported ${inserted} new entries. Total MFE rows: ${count}`);\n} catch (e) {\n  console.error(\"Import failed:\", e instanceof Error ? e.message : e);\n  process.exit(1);\n}",

}
```
