{
  "content": "\"use client\";\nimport { useState } from \"react\";\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { useFetch, apiFetch } from \"@/components/useFetch\";\nimport { Modal } from \"@/components/Modal\";\nimport {\n  Wrench,\n  Search,\n  CheckSquare,\n  FileText,\n  ExternalLink,\n  Sparkles,\n  Loader2,\n  Filter,\n} from \"lucide-react\";\nimport clsx from \"clsx\";\n\ninterface ServiceRow {\n  id: string;\n  title: string;\n  url: string;\n  category: string;\n  subcategory: string | null;\n  type: \"article\" | \"tool\" | \"checklist\";\n  description: string;\n}\n\ninterface ServicesData {\n  rows: ServiceRow[];\n  facets: {\n    categories: Record<string, number>;\n    types: Record<string, number>;\n  };\n  total: number;\n}\n\nconst TYPE_META: Record<string, { label: string; icon: typeof FileText; color: string }> = {\n  article: { label: \"Articles\", icon: FileText, color: \"text-sky-500\" },\n  tool: { label: \"Tools\", icon: Wrench, color: \"text-violet-500\" },\n  checklist: { label: \"Checklists\", icon: CheckSquare, color: \"text-emerald-500\" },\n};\n\nexport default function ServicesPage() {\n  const [category, setCategory] = useState(\"all\");\n  const [type, setType] = useState(\"all\");\n  const [q, setQ] = useState(\"\");\n  const [active, setActive] = useState<ServiceRow | null>(null);\n  const [plan, setPlan] = useState<string[] | null>(null);\n  const [planLoading, setPlanLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [planError, setPlanError] = useState(\"\");\n\n  const url = `/api/services?category=${encodeURIComponent(category)}&type=${encodeURIComponent(type)}&q=${encodeURIComponent(q)}`;\n  const { data, loading } = useFetch<ServicesData>(url);\n\n  const rows = data?.rows || [];\n  const cats = data?.facets?.categories || {};\n  const types = data?.facets?.types || {};\n\n  async function generatePlan(svc: ServiceRow) {\n    setActive(svc);\n    setPlan(null);\n    setPlanError(\"\");\n    setPlanLoading(true);\n    try {\n      const r = (await apiFetch(`/api/services/${svc.id}/run`, {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ action: \"action-plan\" }),\n      })) as { steps: string[]; error?: string };\n      if (r.error) setPlanError(r.error);\n      else setPlan(r.steps);\n    } catch (e) {\n      setPlanError(e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\");\n    } finally {\n      setPlanLoading(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"p-6 max-w-7xl mx-auto\">\n      <header className=\"mb-6\">\n        <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-semibold flex items-center gap-2\">\n          <Wrench className=\"w-6 h-6 text-violet-500\" />\n          Marketing Services\n        </h1>\n        <p className=\"muted text-sm mt-1\">\n          {data?.total || 0} actionable tactics, tools, and checklists — the largest marketing services catalog in the hub.\n        </p>\n      </header>\n\n      {/* Search */}\n      <div className=\"flex gap-2 mb-4\">\n        <div className=\"relative flex-1\">\n          <Search className=\"w-4 h-4 absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 muted\" />\n          <input\n            value={q}\n            onChange={(e) => setQ(e.target.value)}\n            placeholder=\"Search services…\"\n            className=\"w-full pl-9 pr-3 py-2 rounded-lg border border-[rgb(var(--border-strong))] bg-[rgb(var(--surface))] text-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-violet-500/30\"\n          />\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Type tabs */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 mb-3 flex-wrap\">\n        {[\"all\", \"tool\", \"checklist\", \"article\"].map((t) => {\n          const Icon = t === \"all\" ? Filter : t === \"tool\" ? Wrench : t === \"checklist\" ? CheckSquare : FileText;\n          const count = t === \"all\" ? data?.total || 0 : types[t] || 0;\n          return (\n            <button\n              key={t}\n              onClick={() => setType(t)}\n              className={clsx(\n                \"px-3 py-1.5 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium flex items-center gap-1.5 transition-colors\",\n                type === t\n                  ? \"bg-violet-500/10 text-violet-600 dark:text-violet-400 border border-violet-500/30\"\n                  : \"muted hover:bg-[rgb(var(--hover))] border border-transparent\",\n              )}\n            >\n              <Icon className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n              {t === \"all\" ? \"All\" : TYPE_META[t]?.label || t}\n              <span className=\"text-xs muted\">{count}</span>\n            </button>\n          );\n        })}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Category pills */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center gap-1.5 mb-5 flex-wrap\">\n        <button\n          onClick={() => setCategory(\"all\")}\n          className={clsx(\n            \"px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md text-xs font-semibold transition-colors\",\n            category === \"all\" ? \"bg-[rgb(var(--hover))] text-[rgb(var(--text))]\" : \"muted hover:bg-[rgb(var(--hover))]\",\n          )}\n        >\n          All ({data?.total || 0})\n        </button>\n        {Object.entries(cats).map(([cat, count]) => (\n          <button\n            key={cat}\n            onClick={() => setCategory(cat)}\n            className={clsx(\n              \"px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md text-xs font-semibold transition-colors truncate max-w-[200px]\",\n              category === cat ? \"bg-[rgb(var(--hover))] text-[rgb(var(--text))]\" : \"muted hover:bg-[rgb(var(--hover))]\",\n            )}\n            title={cat}\n          >\n            {cat} ({count})\n          </button>\n        ))}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Grid */}\n      {loading ? (\n        <div className=\"flex items-center justify-center py-20 muted\">\n          <Loader2 className=\"w-5 h-5 animate-spin mr-2\" /> Loading services…\n        </div>\n      ) : rows.length === 0 ? (\n        <div className=\"text-center py-20 muted\">No services match your filters.</div>\n      ) : (\n        <div className=\"grid gap-3 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3\">\n          {rows.map((svc) => {\n            const Icon = TYPE_META[svc.type]?.icon || FileText;\n            const color = TYPE_META[svc.type]?.color || \"text-slate-500\";\n            return (\n              <div\n                key={svc.id}\n                className=\"rounded-xl border border-[rgb(var(--border-strong))] bg-[rgb(var(--surface))] p-4 hover:shadow-md transition-shadow flex flex-col\"\n              >\n                <div className=\"flex items-start gap-2 mb-2\">\n                  <Icon className={clsx(\"w-5 h-5 flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5\", color)} />\n                  <div className=\"min-w-0 flex-1\">\n                    <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-sm leading-snug line-clamp-2\">{svc.title}</h3>\n                    <span className=\"text-xs muted\">{svc.category}</span>\n                  </div>\n                </div>\n                {svc.description && (\n                  <p className=\"text-xs muted line-clamp-2 mb-3 flex-1\">{svc.description}</p>\n                )}\n                <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 mt-auto\">\n                  {svc.type === \"tool\" && (\n                    <Link\n                      href={`/services/tool/${svc.id}`}\n                      className=\"text-xs font-semibold px-2 py-1 rounded-md bg-violet-500/10 text-violet-600 dark:text-violet-400 hover:bg-violet-500/20\"\n                    >\n                      Launch Tool\n                    </Link>\n                  )}\n                  {svc.type === \"checklist\" && (\n                    <Link\n                      href={`/services/checklist/${svc.id}`}\n                      className=\"text-xs font-semibold px-2 py-1 rounded-md bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-600 dark:text-emerald-400 hover:bg-emerald-500/20\"\n                    >\n                      Run Checklist\n                    </Link>\n                  )}\n                  <button\n                    onClick={() => generatePlan(svc)}\n                    className=\"text-xs font-semibold px-2 py-1 rounded-md bg-sky-500/10 text-sky-600 dark:text-sky-400 hover:bg-sky-500/20 flex items-center gap-1\"\n                  >\n                    <Sparkles className=\"w-3 h-3\" /> AI Plan\n                  </button>\n                  {svc.url && (\n                    <a\n                      href={svc.url}\n                      target=\"_blank\"\n                      rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\n                      className=\"text-xs muted hover:text-[rgb(var(--text))] flex items-center gap-1 ml-auto\"\n                    >\n                      <ExternalLink className=\"w-3 h-3\" />\n                    </a>\n                  )}\n                </div>\n              </div>\n            );\n          })}\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {/* AI Action Plan Modal */}\n      <Modal\n        open={!!active}\n        onClose={() => { setActive(null); setPlan(null); setPlanError(\"\"); }}\n        title=\"AI Action Plan\"\n      >\n        {active && (\n          <div className=\"space-y-3\">\n            <div>\n              <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-sm\">{active.title}</h3>\n              <p className=\"text-xs muted\">{active.category} · {active.type}</p>\n            </div>\n            {planLoading && (\n              <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 muted text-sm py-4\">\n                <Loader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" /> Generating actionable steps…\n              </div>\n            )}\n            {planError && (\n              <div className=\"text-sm text-rose-500 py-2\">{planError}</div>\n            )}\n            {plan && (\n              <ol className=\"space-y-2\">\n                {plan.map((step, i) => (\n                  <li key={i} className=\"flex items-start gap-2 text-sm\">\n                    <span className=\"flex-shrink-0 w-5 h-5 rounded-full bg-violet-500/10 text-violet-600 dark:text-violet-400 text-xs font-bold flex items-center justify-center\">\n                      {i + 1}\n                    </span>\n                    <span>{step}</span>\n                  </li>\n                ))}\n              </ol>\n            )}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </Modal>\n    </div>\n  );\n}",

}

  "content": "\"use client\";\nimport { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { useFetch } from \"@/components/useFetch\";\nimport { Wrench, ExternalLink, ArrowLeft, Loader2 } from \"lucide-react\";\n\ninterface ServiceRow {\n  id: string;\n  title: string;\n  url: string;\n  category: string;\n  type: string;\n  description: string;\n  meta: Record<string, unknown>;\n}\n\nexport default function ToolExecutorPage({ params }: { params: { id: string } }) {\n  const { data: svc, loading } = useFetch<ServiceRow>(`/api/services/${params.id}`);\n  const [inputs, setInputs] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});\n  const [result, setResult] = useState<string | null>(null);\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (svc) {\n      setInputs({ topic: svc.title, goal: \"\" });\n    }\n  }, [svc]);\n\n  async function run() {\n    setBusy(true);\n    setResult(null);\n    try {\n      const r = await fetch(`/api/services/${params.id}/run`, {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ action: \"action-plan\", ...inputs }),\n      });\n      const j = await r.json();\n      if (j.steps) setResult(j.steps.join(\"\\n\"));\n      else setResult(j.error || \"Failed\");\n    } catch (e) {\n      setResult(e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"Failed\");\n    } finally {\n      setBusy(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (loading) return <div className=\"p-6 flex items-center gap-2 muted\"><Loader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" /> Loading…</div>;\n  if (!svc) return <div className=\"p-6 muted\">Tool not found.</div>;\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"p-6 max-w-3xl mx-auto\">\n      <Link href=\"/services\" className=\"text-sm muted hover:text-[rgb(var(--text))] flex items-center gap-1 mb-4\">\n        <ArrowLeft className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> Back to Services\n      </Link>\n      <header className=\"mb-6\">\n        <h1 className=\"text-xl font-semibold flex items-center gap-2\">\n          <Wrench className=\"w-5 h-5 text-violet-500\" />\n          {svc.title}\n        </h1>\n        <p className=\"text-xs muted mt-1\">{svc.category} · Tool</p>\n      </header>\n\n      {svc.description && <p className=\"text-sm muted mb-6\">{svc.description}</p>}\n\n      <div className=\"space-y-4\">\n        <div>\n          <label className=\"text-sm font-semibold block mb-1\">Topic / Focus</label>\n          <input\n            value={inputs.topic || \"\"}\n            onChange={(e) => setInputs({ ...inputs, topic: e.target.value })}\n            className=\"w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-lg border border-[rgb(var(--border-strong))] bg-[rgb(var(--surface))] text-sm\"\n          />\n        </div>\n        <div>\n          <label className=\"text-sm font-semibold block mb-1\">Your Goal (optional)</label>\n          <textarea\n            value={inputs.goal || \"\"}\n            onChange={(e) => setInputs({ ...inputs, goal: e.target.value })}\n            rows={3}\n            placeholder=\"What are you trying to achieve?\"\n            className=\"w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-lg border border-[rgb(var(--border-strong))] bg-[rgb(var(--surface))] text-sm\"\n          />\n        </div>\n        <button\n          onClick={run}\n          disabled={busy}\n          className=\"px-4 py-2 rounded-lg bg-violet-500 text-white text-sm font-semibold hover:bg-violet-600 disabled:opacity-50 flex items-center gap-2\"\n        >\n          {busy ? <Loader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" /> : <Wrench className=\"w-4 h-4\" />}\n          Run Tool\n        </button>\n      </div>\n\n      {result && (\n        <div className=\"mt-6 p-4 rounded-lg border border-[rgb(var(--border-strong))] bg-[rgb(var(--surface))]\">\n          <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-sm mb-2\">Result</h3>\n          <pre className=\"text-sm whitespace-pre-wrap\">{result}</pre>\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {svc.url && (\n        <a\n          href={svc.url}\n          target=\"_blank\"\n          rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\n          className=\"mt-6 inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-sm text-sky-500 hover:underline\"\n        >\n          <ExternalLink className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> View original resource\n        </a>\n      )}\n    </div>\n  );\n}",

Now the checklist executor page.

**tool `write`** completed
```input
{
  "content": "\"use client\";\nimport { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { useFetch, apiFetch } from \"@/components/useFetch\";\nimport { CheckSquare, ArrowLeft, Loader2, Check, Circle, Sparkles } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport clsx from \"clsx\";\n\ninterface ServiceRow {\n  id: string;\n  title: string;\n  url: string;\n  category: string;\n  type: string;\n  description: string;\n}\n\ninterface RunStep {\n  label: string;\n  done: boolean;\n}\n\nexport default function ChecklistExecutorPage({ params }: { params: { id: string } }) {\n  const { data: svc, loading } = useFetch<ServiceRow>(`/api/services/${params.id}`);\n  const [steps, setSteps] = useState<RunStep[]>([]);\n  const [runId, setRunId] = useState<string | null>(null);\n  const [starting, setStarting] = useState(false);\n  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!svc) return;\n    let cancelled = false;\n    (async () => {\n      setStarting(true);\n      try {\n        const r = await apiFetch(`/api/services/${params.id}/run`, {\n          method: \"POST\",\n          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({ action: \"start\" }),\n        }) as { id: string; steps: RunStep[] };\n        if (!cancelled) {\n          setRunId(r.id);\n          setSteps(r.steps);\n        }\n      } catch {\n        // fallback to static steps\n        if (!cancelled) setSteps([{ label: \"Review this checklist\", done: false }]);\n      } finally {\n        if (!cancelled) setStarting(false);\n      }\n    })();\n    return () => { cancelled = true; };\n  }, [svc]);\n\n  async function toggle(i: number) {\n    const next = steps.map((s, idx) => idx === i ? { ...s, done: !s.done } : s);\n    setSteps(next);\n    if (runId) {\n      setSaving(true);\n      try {\n        await apiFetch(`/api/services/${params.id}/run`, {\n          method: \"PATCH\",\n          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n          body: JSON.stringify({ run_id: runId, steps: next }),\n        });\n      } catch { /* ignore */ } finally {\n        setSaving(false);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  const completed = steps.filter((s) => s.done).length;\n  const pct = steps.length ? Math.round((completed / steps.length) * 100) : 0;\n\n  if (loading) return <div className=\"p-6 flex items-center gap-2 muted\"><Loader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" /> Loading…</div>;\n  if (!svc) return <div className=\"p-6 muted\">Checklist not found.</div>;\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"p-6 max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n      <Link href=\"/services\" className=\"text-sm muted hover:text-[rgb(var(--text))] flex items-center gap-1 mb-4\">\n        <ArrowLeft className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> Back to Services\n      </Link>\n      <header className=\"mb-6\">\n        <h1 className=\"text-xl font-semibold flex items-center gap-2\">\n          <CheckSquare className=\"w-5 h-5 text-emerald-500\" />\n          {svc.title}\n        </h1>\n        <p className=\"text-xs muted mt-1\">{svc.category} · Checklist</p>\n      </header>\n\n      {svc.description && <p className=\"text-sm muted mb-4\">{svc.description}</p>}\n\n      {/* Progress bar */}\n      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n        <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between text-sm mb-1\">\n          <span className=\"font-semibold\">{completed} / {steps.length} complete</span>\n          <span className=\"muted\">{pct}%</span>\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"h-2 rounded-full bg-slate-200 dark:bg-zinc-800 overflow-hidden\">\n          <div className=\"h-full bg-emerald-500 transition-all\" style={{ width: `${pct}%` }} />\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      {starting ? (\n        <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 muted text-sm py-8\">\n          <Loader2 className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" /> Generating checklist steps…\n        </div>\n      ) : (\n        <div className=\"space-y-2\">\n          {steps.map((step, i) => (\n            <button\n              key={i}\n              onClick={() => toggle(i)}\n              className={clsx(\n                \"w-full text-left flex items-start gap-3 p-3 rounded-lg border transition-colors\",\n                step.done\n                  ? \"border-emerald-500/30 bg-emerald-500/5\"\n                  : \"border-[rgb(var(--border-strong))] hover:bg-[rgb(var(--hover))]\",\n              )}\n            >\n              {step.done ? (\n                <Check className=\"w-5 h-5 text-emerald-500 flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5\" />\n              ) : (\n                <Circle className=\"w-5 h-5 muted flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5\" />\n              )}\n              <span className={clsx(\"text-sm\", step.done && \"line-through muted\")}>{step.label}</span>\n            </button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {saving && <p className=\"text-xs muted mt-3 flex items-center gap-1\"><Loader2 className=\"w-3 h-3 animate-spin\" /> Saving…</p>}\n      {pct === 100 && steps.length > 0 && (\n        <div className=\"mt-4 p-3 rounded-lg bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-600 dark:text-emerald-400 text-sm font-semibold flex items-center gap-2\">\n          <Sparkles className=\"w-4 h-4\" /> Checklist complete!\n        </div>\n      )}\n    </div>\n  );\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/app/services/checklist/[id]/page.tsx"
}

The valuable approach for Phase 3 is a **tool-to-hub mapping layer** that connects each external tool to the corresponding hub capability, so users discover both the external tool and the native equivalent.

```input
{
  "content": "/**\n * Tool-to-Hub mapping — connects MFE external tool entries to native hub\n * surfaces so the tool executor can deep-link users into the right module.\n *\n * Keyed by lowercased title fragment. Falls back to category-based routing.\n */\nexport interface ToolMapping {\n  match: string[];\n  hubPath?: string;\n  hubLabel?: string;\n  categoryFallback?: string;\n}\n\nconst MAPPINGS: ToolMapping[] = [\n  { match: [\"buffer\", \"hootsuite\", \"later\", \"statusbrew\", \"dlvr.it\"], hubPath: \"/social\", hubLabel: \"Social Studio\" },\n  { match: [\"canva\", \"pablo\"], hubPath: \"/studio\", hubLabel: \"Studio\" },\n  { match: [\"grammarly\", \"hemingway\", \"ginger\", \"readable\", \"ru-checker\"], hubPath: \"/copy\", hubLabel: \"Copy Generator\" },\n  { match: [\"google analytics campaign url builder\", \"utm\"], hubPath: \"/ads\", hubLabel: \"Ads Manager\" },\n  { match: [\"really good emails\", \"email copy\", \"email sequences\", \"email drip\"], hubPath: \"/emails\", hubLabel: \"Email Builder\" },\n  { match: [\"norbert\", \"pipl\", \"streak\", \"gmass\"], hubPath: \"/sequences\", hubLabel: \"Sequences\" },\n  { match: [\"similarweb\", \"alexa\", \"buzzsumo\", \"siftery\", \"alternativeto\"], hubPath: \"/competitive-intelligence\", hubLabel: \"Competitive Intel\" },\n  { match: [\"klear\", \"twitonomy\", \"followerwonk\", \"twitter follower\", \"snoop\", \"scoutzen\"], hubPath: \"/influencer\", hubLabel: \"Influencer\" },\n  { match: [\"typeform\", \"form\"], hubPath: \"/forms\", hubLabel: \"Forms\" },\n  { match: [\"product hunt\", \"hunters\", \"leaderboard\"], hubPath: \"/launch\", hubLabel: \"Launch Planning\" },\n  { match: [\"reddit\"], hubPath: \"/social\", hubLabel: \"Social Studio\" },\n  { match: [\"linkedin\"], hubPath: \"/outreach\", hubLabel: \"Outreach\" },\n  { match: [\"crisp\", \"live chat\"], hubPath: \"/inbox\", hubLabel: \"Inbox\" },\n  { match: [\"onboard\"], hubPath: \"/lifecycle\", hubLabel: \"Lifecycle\" },\n  { match: [\"google advanced search\", \"search operators\"], hubPath: \"/seo\", hubLabel: \"SEO Toolkit\" },\n];\n\nconst CATEGORY_FALLBACK: Record<string, string> = {\n  \"Social Media Marketing\": \"/social\",\n  \"Content Marketing\": \"/content\",\n  \"Lifecycle Email Marketing\": \"/emails\",\n  \"Cold Email Marketing\": \"/sequences\",\n  \"User Research\": \"/personas\",\n  \"Influencer Marketing\": \"/influencer\",\n  \"Market Research\": \"/competitive-intelligence\",\n  \"Marketing for Product Hunt Launch\": \"/launch\",\n};\n\nexport function mapToolToHub(title: string, category: string): { hubPath?: string; hubLabel?: string } {\n  const lower = title.toLowerCase();\n  for (const m of MAPPINGS) {\n    if (m.match.some((kw) => lower.includes(kw))) {\n      return { hubPath: m.hubPath, hubLabel: m.hubLabel };\n    }\n  }\n  const fallback = CATEGORY_FALLBACK[category];\n  if (fallback) return { hubPath: fallback, hubLabel: undefined };\n  return {};\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/services/tool-map.ts"
}
```

### [assistant] 2026-07-14T13:45:13.069000+00:00  @glm-5.2


> _reasoning_: The audit is comprehensive. Let me summarize the key findings and present them clearly to the user.

Based on a thorough audit of the entire `marketing-hub` (209 pages, 320 API routes, 124 tables), here's what's needed for a complete product:

## Already Solid ✅
- Public site (landing, pricing, blog, case studies, enterprise, developers)
- Auth stack (sessions, RBAC, 2FA, SSO/OIDC, magic links, CSRF, rate-limiting, audit log)
- Core CRUD: CRM, campaigns, content, email, SEO, ads, funnels, forms, segments, experiments, finance, orchestrator
- Cockpit dashboard, settings (9 tabs), billing abstraction, unified AI provider, SQLite→Supabase adapter

## Critical Gaps for Production Readiness

| # | Gap | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **5,333 TypeScript errors** suppressed by `ignoreBuildErrors` — build silently ships type-unsafe code | Critical |
| 2 | **No test suite** — only 1 ad-hoc `node:test` file, no runner, no CI checks | Critical |
| 3 | **No CI/CD** — only a keepalive cron; no build/lint/test/typecheck on PRs | High |
| 4 | **56 seed-surface stubs** masquerade as full features (manual CRUD only, `// TODO: ingest adapter`) | High |
| 5 | **`/signup` is a stub** — not a real signup page; real onboarding is at `/setup` | High |
| 6 | **Duplicate route trees** — `api/dive/dive/`, `api/triangle/triangle/`, `api/embed/embed/`, etc. (orphaned copies) | Medium |
| 7 | **No real Stripe SDK** — billing uses raw fetch (no webhook signature verification) | High |
| 8 | **No email-sending SDK** — outbox exists but sender is env-stubbed | High |
| 9 | **Ad providers have `TODO LIVE:`** — Google/Meta OAuth token refresh + conversion upload are stubs | Medium |
| 10 | **Dev auto-auth bypass** — `middleware.ts` injects a hardcoded session in any non-prod env | High |
| 11 | **No error monitoring** (Sentry) or product analytics (PostHog) | Medium |
| 12 | **No Dockerfile** — Vercel-only deployment | Low |
| 13 | **3 fragmented onboarding endpoints** — need consolidation into one first-run wizard | Medium |

## Recommended Priority Order
1. Fix the duplicate orphan routes (quick cleanup, immediate)
2. Fix `/signup` conflict + consolidate onboarding
3. Install `stripe` SDK + wire real webhook signatures
4. Add `vitest` + a test suite + CI workflow
5. Chip away at the 5,333 TS errors (start with the modules that have real runtime bugs)
6. Secure the dev auto-auth bypass
7. Add real email sending (Resend/SendGrid)
8. Add Sentry + PostHog

Want me to start on any of these, or tackle them in priority order?

/**
 * Public onboarding — POST /api/onboard
 *
 *   { name, email, password, workspace_name?, workspace_slug? }
 *
 * Creates the user, creates a new workspace (unless "default"), makes
 * the user its owner, logs them in.
 */
import { NextRequest } from "next/server";
import { json, readJson, err } from "@/lib/api-helpers";
import { db, uid, now } from "@/lib/db";
import bcrypt from "bcryptjs";
import { createWorkspace, DEFAULT_WORKSPACE } from "@/lib/workspace";
import { createSession, setSessionCookie, getUserCount } from "@/lib/auth";

export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  const body = await readJson<Record<string, unknown>>(req);
  if (!body?.email || !body?.password || !body?.name) {
    return err("name, email, password required", 400);
  }
  if (getUserCount() > 0)
    return err(
      "Onboarding only allowed when no users exist yet. Use /api/auth/signup if you have an invite.",
      403,
    );

  const email = body.email.trim().toLowerCase();
  if (!/^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$/.test(email)) return err("Invalid email", 400);

The three endpoints serve different purposes:
- `/api/onboard` — first-run account creation (when no users exist)
- `/api/onboarding` — step-by-step workspace setup (POST step updates)
- `/api/onboarding-checklist` — 7-day onboarding checklist (GET shows progress)

1: /**
2:  * Billing — provider-agnostic abstraction over Stripe and Revolut.
3:  *
4:  * Design goals:
5:  *   1. The Hub can be sold via card (Stripe) OR bank-direct (Revolut Merchant).
6:  *   2. The rest of the app doesn't care which provider the customer uses.
7:  *   3. Webhooks from both providers land in /api/billing/webhook and
8:  *      normalize into the same `billing_events` log.
9:  *
10:  * Provider config (env vars):
11:  *   STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...

### [assistant] 2026-07-14T14:17:41.287000+00:00  @glm-5.2


Now replace the raw-fetch `stripeCheckout` with the SDK version.

**tool `edit`** completed
```input
{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/billing.ts",
  "newString": "export async function stripeCheckout(body: StripeCheckoutBody): Promise<CheckoutSession> {\n  const stripe = getStripe();\n  if (!stripe) throw new Error(\"Stripe not configured (STRIPE_SECRET_KEY missing)\");\n  const priceId =\n    body.plan === \"agency\" ? process.env.STRIPE_PRICE_AGENCY : process.env.STRIPE_PRICE_PROD;\n  if (!priceId) throw new Error(`Missing STRIPE_PRICE_${body.plan.toUpperCase()}`);\n\n  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({\n    mode: \"subscription\",\n    line_items: [{ price: priceId, quantity: 1 }],\n    success_url: body.success_url,\n    cancel_url: body.cancel_url,\n    client_reference_id: body.workspace_id,\n    metadata: { workspace_id: body.workspace_id, plan: body.plan },\n    customer_email: body.customer_email,\n  });\n  return { url: session.url || \"\", provider: \"stripe\", session_id: session.id };\n}",
  "oldString": "export async function stripeCheckout(body: StripeCheckoutBody): Promise<CheckoutSession> {\n  const key = process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY;\n  if (!key) throw new Error(\"Stripe not configured (STRIPE_SECRET_KEY missing)\");\n  const priceId =\n    body.plan === \"agency\" ? process.env.STRIPE_PRICE_AGENCY : process.env.STRIPE_PRICE_PROD;\n  if (!priceId) throw new Error(`Missing STRIPE_PRICE_${body.plan.toUpperCase()}`);\n\n  // Minimal Stripe API call using fetch (no SDK dep).\n  const params = new URLSearchParams();\n  params.set(\"mode\", \"subscription\");\n  params.set(\"line_items[0][price]\", priceId);\n  params.set(\"line_items[0][quantity]\", \"1\");\n  params.set(\"success_url\", body.success_url);\n  params.set(\"cancel_url\", body.cancel_url);\n  params.set(\"client_reference_id\", body.workspace_id);\n  params.set(\"metadata[workspace_id]\", body.workspace_id);\n  params.set(\"metadata[plan]\", body.plan);\n  if (body.customer_email) params.set(\"customer_email\", body.customer_email);\n\n  const r = await fetch(\"https://api.stripe.com/v1/checkout/sessions\", {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers: {\n      Authorization: `Bearer ${key}`,\n      \"Content-Type\": \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\",\n      \"Stripe-Version\": \"2024-06-20\",\n    },\n    body: params.toString(),\n  });\n  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`Stripe checkout error: ${r.status} ${await r.text()}`);\n  const data = (await r.json()) as Record<string, unknown>;\n  return { url: data.url, provider: \"stripe\", session_id: data.id };\n}"
}

{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/billing.ts",
  "newString": "export async function stripePortal(\n  customerId: string,\n  returnUrl: string,\n): Promise<BillingPortalSession> {\n  const stripe = getStripe();\n  if (!stripe) throw new Error(\"Stripe not configured\");\n  const session = await stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({\n    customer: customerId,\n    return_url: returnUrl,\n  });\n  return { url: session.url, provider: \"stripe\" };\n}",
  "oldString": "export async function stripePortal(\n  customerId: string,\n  returnUrl: string,\n): Promise<BillingPortalSession> {\n  const key = process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY;\n  if (!key) throw new Error(\"Stripe not configured\");\n  const params = new URLSearchParams();\n  params.set(\"customer\", customerId);\n  params.set(\"return_url\", returnUrl);\n  const r = await fetch(\"https://api.stripe.com/v1/billing_portal/sessions\", {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers: {\n      Authorization: `Bearer ${key}`,\n      \"Content-Type\": \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\",\n      \"Stripe-Version\": \"2024-06-20\",\n    },\n    body: params.toString(),\n  });\n  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`Stripe portal error: ${r.status} ${await r.text()}`);\n  const data = (await r.json()) as Record<string, unknown>;\n  return { url: data.url, provider: \"stripe\" };\n}"
}

Now replace the manual webhook verification with the Stripe SDK's `webhooks.constructEvent`.

**tool `edit`** completed
```input
{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/billing.ts",
  "newString": "export function verifyStripeWebhook(rawBody: string, signature: string | null): Record<string, unknown> | null {\n  const secret = process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  const stripe = getStripe();\n  if (!stripe || !secret || !signature) return null;\n  try {\n    const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, signature, secret);\n    return event as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}",
  "oldString": "export function verifyStripeWebhook(rawBody: string, signature: string | null): Record<string, unknown> | null {\n  const secret = process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (!secret || !signature) return null;\n  // Stripe sends \"t=...,v1=...\"\n  const parts = signature.split(\",\").reduce((acc: Record<string, string>, p: string) => {\n    const [k, v] = p.split(\"=\");\n    acc[k] = v;\n    return acc;\n  }, {});\n  if (!parts.t || !parts.v1) return null;\n  const crypto = require(\"node:crypto\");\n  const signedPayload = `${parts.t}.${rawBody}`;\n  const expected = crypto.createHmac(\"sha256\", secret).update(signedPayload).digest(\"hex\");\n  if (!crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(parts.v1), Buffer.from(expected))) return null;\n  return JSON.parse(rawBody);\n}"
}

1: /**
2:  * Email orchestrator — the single entry point for sending.
3:  *
4:  * Rules:
5:  *   1. ALWAYS write to email_outbox first. The marketer's record of truth.
6:  *   2. If a provider is configured AND the message has no scheduled_for in the future,
7:  *      try to deliver through the provider. Mark outbox row with the result.
8:  *   3. If provider fails, the row stays in 'queued' so the scheduler can retry.
9:  *   4. If no provider is configured, the row goes straight to 'sent' with provider='local'
10:  *      (visible in /outbox as a locally-recorded send).

/**
 * Provider registry — one place to look up any provider by id.
 */
import type { EmailProvider } from "./types";
import { smtpProvider } from "./smtp";
import { resendProvider } from "./resend";
import { sendgridProvider } from "./sendgrid";

export const providers: Record<string, EmailProvider> = {
  smtp: smtpProvider,
  resend: resendProvider,
  sendgrid: sendgridProvider,
};

/**
 * List of providers that need an active outbound network connection
 * (we still test SMTP locally but the others always go over the internet).
 */
export const providerList = [smtpProvider, resendProvider, sendgridProvider];

```input
{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/providers/resend.ts",
  "newString": "/**\n * Resend provider — uses the official Resend SDK.\n * Auth: Bearer token\n * Docs: https://resend.com/docs/api-reference/emails/send-email\n */\nimport { Resend } from \"resend\";\nimport type { EmailProvider, ProviderConfig, SendInput, SendResult, TestResult } from \"./types\";\n\ntype ResendConfig = { apiKey: string; from: string };\n\nexport const resendProvider: EmailProvider = {\n  id: \"resend\",\n  displayName: \"Resend\",\n  async test(cfg: ProviderConfig): Promise<TestResult> {\n    const c = cfg as ResendConfig;\n    if (!c.apiKey) return { ok: false, error: \"apiKey required\" };\n    try {\n      const client = new Resend(c.apiKey);\n      const res = await client.apiKeys.list();\n      if (res.error) return { ok: false, error: `Resend rejected key: ${res.error.message}` };\n      return { ok: true, detail: \"Resend API key is valid\" };\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      return { ok: false, error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) };\n    }\n  },\n\n  async send(cfg: ProviderConfig, msg: SendInput): Promise<SendResult> {\n    const c = cfg as ResendConfig;\n    try {\n      const client = new Resend(c.apiKey);\n      const { data, error } = await client.emails.send({\n        from: msg.from || c.from,\n        to: [msg.to],\n        subject: msg.subject,\n        html: msg.html,\n        text: msg.text,\n        reply_to: msg.replyTo,\n        tags: msg.tags\n          ? Object.entries(msg.tags).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }))\n          : undefined,\n      });\n      if (error) {\n        return { ok: false, error: error.message || \"Resend send failed\", raw: error };\n      }\n      if (data?.id) {\n        return { ok: true, providerMessageId: data.id, raw: data };\n      }\n      return { ok: false, error: \"No message ID returned\", raw: data };\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      return { ok: false, error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) };\n    }\n  },\n};",
  "oldString": "/**\n * Resend provider — POST https://api.resend.com/emails\n * Auth: Bearer token\n * Docs: https://resend.com/docs/api-reference/emails/send-email\n */\nimport type { EmailProvider, ProviderConfig, SendInput, SendResult, TestResult } from \"./types\";\n\ntype ResendConfig = { apiKey: string; from: string };\n\nasync function resendFetch(cfg: ResendConfig, path: string, body: Record<string, unknown>) {\n  const res = await fetch(`https://api.resend.com${path}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers: {\n      Authorization: `Bearer ${cfg.apiKey}`,\n      \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    },\n    body: JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n  const text = await res.text();\n  let json: Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n  try {\n    json = JSON.parse(text);\n  } catch {\n    json = { raw: text };\n  }\n  return { ok: res.ok, status: res.status, json };\n}\n\nexport const resendProvider: EmailProvider = {\n  id: \"resend\",\n  displayName: \"Resend\",\n  async test(cfg: ProviderConfig): Promise<TestResult> {\n    const c = cfg as ResendConfig;\n    if (!c.apiKey) return { ok: false, error: \"apiKey required\" };\n    try {\n      // Resend exposes GET /api-keys — use it to verify the key\n      const res = await fetch(\"https://api.resend.com/api-keys\", {\n        headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${c.apiKey}` },\n      });\n      if (res.ok) return { ok: true, detail: \"Resend API key is valid\" };\n      const text = await res.text();\n      return { ok: false, error: `Resend rejected key (${res.status}): ${text.slice(0, 200)}` };\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      return { ok: false, error: e.message };\n    }\n  },\n\n  async send(cfg: ProviderConfig, msg: SendInput): Promise<SendResult> {\n    const c = cfg as ResendConfig;\n    const r = await resendFetch(c, \"/emails\", {\n      from: msg.from || c.from,\n      to: [msg.to],\n      subject: msg.subject,\n      html: msg.html,\n      text: msg.text,\n      reply_to: msg.replyTo ? [msg.replyTo] : undefined,\n      tags: msg.tags\n        ? Object.entries(msg.tags).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }))\n        : undefined,\n    });\n    if (r.ok && r.json?.id) return { ok: true, providerMessageId: r.json.id, raw: r.json };\n    return {\n      ok: false,\n      error: r.json?.message || r.json?.error?.message || `HTTP ${r.status}`,\n      raw: r.json,\n    };\n  },\n};"
}

expect(inserted).toBe(1);\n    const inserted2 = importServices([{ title: \"Test Idempotent Entry\", category: \"Test\", type: \"article\" }], \"test-idempotent\");\n    expect(inserted2).toBe(0);\n === 0) return;\n    const svcId = list.rows[0]!.id;\n    const runId = createRun(svcId, [\n      { label: \"Step 1\", done: false },\n      { label: \"Step 2\", done: false },\n    ]);\n    expect(runId).toBeTruthy();\n\n    const runs = listRuns(svcId);\n    expect(runs.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n\n    updateRun(runId, [\n      { label: \"Step 1\", done: true },\n      { label: \"Step   "content": "import { describe, it, expect } from \"vitest\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { SEED_SURFACES } from \"@/lib/seed-surfaces\";\nimport { ensureSeedSurface, listSeed, createSeed } from \"@/lib/seed-surface\";\n\ndescribe(\"seed-surface framework\", () => {\n  it(\"all configured surfaces have valid slugs\", () => {\n    for (const s of SEED_SURFACES) {\n      expect(s.slug).toMatch(/^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/);\n      expect(s.label).toBeTruthy();\n      expect(s.statuses.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n      expect(s.categories.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"no duplicate slugs in registry\", () => {\n    const slugs = SEED_SURFACES.map((s) => s.slug);\n    const unique = new Set(slugs);\n    expect(unique.size).toBe(slugs.length);\n  });\n\n  it(\"ensureSeedSurface creates table and seeds rows\", () => {\n    const cfg = SEED_SURFACES[0]!;\n    ensureSeedSurface(cfg);\n    const rows = listSeed(cfg.slug, undefined, 10);\n    expect(rows.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"createSeed inserts a row\", () => {\n    const cfg = SEED_SURFACES[0]!;\n    ensureSeedSurface(cfg);\n    const before = listSeed(cfg.slug, undefined, 1000).length;\n    const id = createSeed(cfg.slug, { title: \"Test Created Row\", status: cfg.statuses[0], category: cfg.categories[0] });\n    expect(id).toBeTruthy();\n    const after = listSeed(cfg.slug, undefined, 1000).length;\n    expect(after).toBe(before + 1);\n  });\n});",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/tests/seed-surface.test.ts"
}

### [assistant] 2026-07-14T14:23:56.371000+00:00  @glm-5.2


The SQL has 9 columns but the VALUES has `?` for id, service_id, workspace_id, user_id, steps, then `'in_progress'`, `NULL`, then `?`, `?` — that's 6 `?` + 2 literals = 8 placeholders for 9 columns. Wait, let me count: `(id, service_id, workspace_id, user_id, steps, status, completed_at, created_at, updated_at)` = 9 columns. VALUES: `?,?,?,?,?,?,'in_progress',NULL,?,?` = 6 params + 'in_progress' + NULL + 2 params = 8 params total. The `.run()` call passes 6 values: `id, serviceId, workspaceId || null, userId || null, JSON.stringify(steps), t, t` = 7 values.

The mismatch: 6 `?` in first part + 2 `?` in last part = 8 `?` total, but we pass 7 values. And the SQL has 9 columns, 8 placeholders + 1 literal = 9 values. Let me recount the `.run()` call: `id, serviceId, workspaceId || null, userId || null, JSON.stringify(steps), t, t` — that's 7 args. But we need 8 `?` matches. We're missing one. The `completed_at` is `NULL` literal, so columns are: id(?), service_id(?), workspace_id(?), user_id(?), steps(?), status('in_progress'), completed_at(NULL), created_at(?), updated_at(?). That's 6 `?`. The run call passes: id, serviceId, workspaceId, userId, steps, t, t = 7 values. So we have 6 `?` but 7 args. The extra `t` is the problem.

This is a security risk — if someone deploys with `NODE_ENV=development` or if the env var is misconfigured, anyone gets full access. Let me secure it by gating it behind an explicit env var.

/**
 * Google Ads LIVE provider — uses the real google-ads-api SDK.
 *
 * This file is ONLY imported when hasAllCreds() returns true. Until then,
 * the dispatch entrypoint (google-ads.ts) returns mock data. This means
 * the SDK never loads on dev machines without credentials.
 *
 * To enable: set in .env.local or in the account config (Connect modal):
 *   GOOGLE_ADS_DEVELOPER_TOKEN
 *   GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_ID
 *   GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_SECRET
 *   GOOGLE_ADS_REFRESH_TOKEN
 *
 * Then in the hub, open /ads/orchestrate, click "Connect account", paste
 * the same values as JSON config. The provider detects them via isLive()
 * and routes here automatically.

  NormalizedAdGroup,

    client_secret: cfg.client_secret as string,
    refresh_token: cfg.refresh_token as string,
    // login_customer_id is required when managing sub-accounts under an MCC
    login_customer_id: cfg.login_customer_id as string | undefined,
  });
}

### [assistant] 2026-07-14T14:29:00.690000+00:00  @glm-5.2


**tool `edit`** completed
```input
{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/providers/google-ads.ts",
  "newString": "  async oauthExchange(cfg, code, redirectUri) {\n    if (!cfg.client_id || !cfg.client_secret) {\n      return { ok: false, error: \"client_id and client_secret required\" };\n    }\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(\"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\" },\n        body: new URLSearchParams({\n          grant_type: \"authorization_code\",\n          client_id: cfg.client_id as string,\n          client_secret: cfg.client_secret as string,\n          code,\n          redirect_uri: redirectUri,\n        }),\n      });\n      const data = await res.json() as Record<string, unknown>;\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        return { ok: false, error: `Google OAuth error: ${data.error_description || data.error || res.status}` };\n      }\n      return {\n        ok: true,\n        refresh_token: data.refresh_token as string,\n        access_token: data.access_token as string,\n      };\n    } catch (e) {\n      return { ok: false, error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : \"OAuth exchange failed\" };\n    }\n  },\n};",
  "oldString": "  async oauthExchange(_cfg, _code, _redirectUri) {\n    // TODO LIVE: POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token with grant_type=authorization_code\n    return { ok: false, error: \"Live OAuth not yet wired. SDK pending.\" };\n  },\n};"

{
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/instrumentation.ts",
  "newString": "export async function register() {\n  if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME === \"nodejs\") {\n    // Sentry error monitoring (optional — env-gated)\n    if (process.env.SENTRY_DSN) {\n      try {\n        const Sentry = await import(\"@sentry/node\");\n        Sentry.init({\n          dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN,\n          environment: process.env.NODE_ENV,\n          tracesSampleRate: parseFloat(process.env.SENTRY_TRACES_SAMPLE_RATE || \"0.1\"),\n        });\n      } catch { /* @sentry/node not installed — skip silently */ }\n    }\n\n    const { startScheduler } = await import(\"./lib/scheduler\");\n    startScheduler();\n    // Register graceful shutdown cleanup for SQLite\n    await import(\"./lib/cleanup\");\n  }\n}",
  "oldString": "export async function register() {\n  if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME === \"nodejs\") {\n    const { startScheduler } = await import(\"./lib/scheduler\");\n    startScheduler();\n    // Register graceful shutdown cleanup for SQLite\n    await import(\"./lib/cleanup\");\n  }\n}"

### [assistant] 2026-07-14T14:30:40.987000+00:00  @glm-5.2


Now #11 — versioned migration system. Instead of replacing the existing self-healing migrations (which would be a massive refactor), I'll add a proper migration runner alongside it that tracks applied migrations.

**tool `write`** completed
```input
{
  "content": "/**\n * Versioned migration runner — tracks applied migrations in a\n * `schema_migrations` table and runs only new ones in order.\n *\n * This supplements (does not replace) the existing self-healing\n * CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS migrations in db/index.ts. As the project\n * matures, individual ALTER TABLE / data migration steps move here.\n */\nimport { db } from \"./db\";\n\nexport interface Migration {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  up: string[];\n}\n\nconst MIGRATIONS: Migration[] = [\n  {\n    version: 1,\n    name: \"initial_schema_migrations_table\",\n    up: [\n      `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (\n        version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n        name TEXT NOT NULL,\n        applied_at INTEGER NOT NULL\n      )`,\n    ],\n  },\n  {\n    version: 2,\n    name: \"marketing_services_tables\",\n    up: [\n      `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS marketing_services (\n        id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        title TEXT NOT NULL,\n        url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        category TEXT NOT NULL,\n        subcategory TEXT,\n        type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'article',\n        description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'mfe',\n        meta TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',\n        created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,\n        updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL\n      )`,\n      `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_marketing_services_category ON marketing_services(category)`,\n      `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_marketing_services_type ON marketing_services(type)`,\n      `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS marketing_service_runs (\n        id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        service_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        workspace_id TEXT,\n        user_id TEXT,\n        steps TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',\n        status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'in_progress',\n        completed_at INTEGER,\n        created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,\n        updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL,\n        FOREIGN KEY(service_id) REFERENCES marketing_services(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n      )`,\n      `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_marketing_service_runs_service ON marketing_service_runs(service_id)`,\n      `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_marketing_service_runs_workspace ON marketing_service_runs(workspace_id)`,\n    ],\n  },\n];\n\nexport function runMigrations(): { applied: number; skipped: number } {\n  db.exec(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (\n    version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n    name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    applied_at INTEGER NOT NULL\n  )`);\n\n  const applied = new Set(\n    (db.prepare(`SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`).all() as { version: number }[]).map(\n      (r) => r.version,\n    ),\n  );\n\n  let newlyApplied = 0;\n  for (const m of MIGRATIONS) {\n    if (applied.has(m.version)) continue;\n    const t = Date.now();\n    db.exec(\"BEGIN\");\n    try {\n      for (const sql of m.up) {\n        db.exec(sql);\n      }\n      db.prepare(`INSERT INTO schema_migrations (version, name, applied_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`).run(\n        m.version,\n        m.name,\n        t,\n      );\n      db.exec(\"COMMIT\");\n      newlyApplied++;\n    } catch (e) {\n      db.exec(\"ROLLBACK\");\n      throw e;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { applied: newlyApplied, skipped: MIGRATIONS.length - newlyApplied };\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/db/migrate.ts"
}
```

{
  "content": "/**\n * Sales Scripts — Hormozi-style sales call framework.\n *\n * Based on Alex Hormozi's sales call structure from his YouTube content\n * and \"The Million Dollar Weekend\" / Acquisition.com training:\n *\n *   1. Pre-Call Research — know their pain, goals, current situation\n *   2. Pattern Interrupt — break their preoccupation in the first 10 seconds\n *   3. Setting the Frame — \"I'm going to ask questions, then tell you if I can help\"\n *   4. Discovery — pain, impact, desire, current solution\n *   5. Gap Summary — reflect back the gap between where they are and where they want\n *   6. The Stack — present the offer as the bridge (Grand Slam Offer anatomy)\n *   7. Trial Close — \"If I can show you X, would you want to move forward?\"\n *   8. Objection Handling — isolate, validate, reframe, answer\n *   9. Urgency / Close — real deadline, bonus expiry, or cohort close\n *  10. Post-Call — follow-up sequence, onboarding trigger\n *\n * Each script is customizable by industry, product type, and price point.\n */\nimport { db, uid, now } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nexport type ScriptStage =\n  | \"pre_call\"\n  | \"pattern_interrupt\"\n  | \"set_frame\"\n  | \"discovery\"\n  | \"gap_summary\"\n  | \"the_stack\"\n  | \"trial_close\"\n  | \"objection_handling\"\n  | \"close\"\n  | \"post_call\";\n\nexport type Industry = \"saas\" | \"agency\" | \"coaching\" | \"ecommerce\" | \"b2b\" | \"fitness\" | \"realestate\" | \"local\";\nexport type PricePoint = \"low_ticket\" | \"mid_ticket\" | \"high_ticket\" | \"enterprise\";\n\nexport interface ScriptStep {\n  stage: ScriptStage;\n  label: string;\n  prompt: string;\n  example: string;\n  duration_sec: number;\n  required: boolean;\n}\n\nexport interface SalesScript {\n  id: string;\n  workspace_id: string;\n  name: string;\n  industry: Industry;\n  price_point: PricePoint;\n  product_name: string;\n  product_price: string;\n  steps: ScriptStep[];\n  objection_bank: ObjectionResponse[];\n  status: string;\n  created_at: number;\n  updated_at: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ObjectionResponse {\n  objection: string;\n  response: string;\n  reframe: string;\n}\n\nexport interface ScriptRun {\n  id: string;\n  script_id: string;\n  contact_name: string;\n  contact_company: string;\n  outcome: \"pending\" | \"won\" | \"lost\" | \"follow_up\";\n  notes: string;\n  deal_value: number | null;\n  created_at: number;\n}\n\nconst STAGE_ORDER: ScriptStage[] = [\n  \"pre_call\",\n  \"pattern_interrupt\",\n  \"set_frame\",\n  \"discovery\",\n  \"gap_summary\",\n  \"the_stack\",\n  \"trial_close\",\n  \"objection_handling\",\n  \"close\",\n  \"post_call\",\n];\n\nconst DEFAULT_STEPS: Record<PricePoint, ScriptStep[]> = {\n  low_ticket: [\n    { stage: \"pre_call\", label: \"Pre-Call Research\", prompt: \"Review their website, LinkedIn, and any prior touchpoints. Note one specific thing you can reference.\", example: \"Saw your post about Q3 hiring — congratulations on the growth.\", duration_sec: 120, required: true },\n    { stage: \"pattern_interrupt\", label: \"Pattern Interrupt\", prompt: \"Break their preoccupation in 10 seconds. Don't start with 'how are you' — say something unexpected.\", example: \"Most people I talk to in your space are drowning in lead quality issues. Is that what's keeping you up, or is it something else?\", duration_sec: 15, required: true },\n    { stage: \"set_frame\", label: \"Set the Frame\", prompt: \"Tell them how the call will go. Set yourself as the evaluator, not the pitcher.\", example: \"I'm going to ask you a few questions to see if what we do can actually help you. If it can't, I'll tell you. Sound good?\", duration_sec: 20, required: true },\n    { stage: \"discovery\", label: \"Discovery\", prompt: \"Ask: What's the #1 thing not working? What have you tried? What's it costing you?\", example: \"What's the single biggest thing that's not working with your current lead flow?\", duration_sec: 300, required: true },\n    { stage: \"gap_summary\", label: \"Gap Summary\", prompt: \"Reflect back: 'So you're at X, you want Y, and the gap is Z. Did I get that right?'\", example: \"So you're getting 50 leads/month, you want 150, and the gap is that your CPL has tripled. Did I get that right?\", duration_sec: 30, required: true },\n    { stage: \"the_stack\", label: \"The Stack\", prompt: \"Present the offer as the bridge. Lead with the dream outcome, then bonuses, then guarantee.\", example: \"Here's what I'd recommend: [core deliverable] + [bonus 1] + [bonus 2], and if it doesn't work, you don't pay.\", duration_sec: 120, required: true },\n    { stage: \"trial_close\", label: \"Trial Close\", prompt: \"Test the temperature before pushing.\", example: \"If I can show you how to triple your lead volume without increasing ad spend, would you want to move forward today?\", duration_sec: 15, required: true },\n    { stage: \"objection_handling\", label: \"Objection Handling\", prompt: \"Isolate the objection: 'Just to be clear, if we can solve [objection], is there anything else stopping you?'\", example: \"Other than the price, is there anything else that would stop you from saying yes?\", duration_sec: 180, required: false },\n    { stage: \"close\", label: \"Close\", prompt: \"Assume the close. Give the next step.\", example: \"Cool — I'll send the invoice now and you'll get the onboarding link within 5 minutes. What email should I send it to?\", duration_sec: 30, required: true },\n    { stage: \"post_call\", label: \"Post-Call\", prompt: \"Send a summary email within 1 hour. Schedule the next touchpoint.\", example: \"Great call! Here's what we agreed on: [summary]. Next step: [action].\", duration_sec: 60, required: true },\n  ],\n  mid_ticket: [\n    { stage: \"pre_call\", label: \"Pre-Call Research\", prompt: \"Deep research: their business model, revenue model, team size, recent news, competitors. Prepare 3 specific questions.\", example: \"I noticed you pivoted from services to productized consulting last quarter — how's that transition going?\", duration_sec: 300, required: true },\n    { stage: \"pattern_interrupt\", label: \"Pattern Interrupt\", prompt: \"Start with a specific observation that shows you did your homework.\", example: \"Before we start — I spent 20 minutes on your site and I have one question that might save us both time. Can I ask it?\", duration_sec: 15, required: true },\n    { stage: \"set_frame\", label: \"Set the Frame\", prompt: \"Position yourself as an expert doing an assessment, not a salesperson.\", example: \"My goal today is to understand your situation and tell you honestly whether we can help. If we can't, I'll refer you to someone who can. Fair?\", duration_sec: 25, required: true },\n    { stage: \"discovery\", label: \"Deep Discovery\", prompt: \"Use the PIP framework: Pain → Impact → Priority. How big is the problem? What happens if they don't fix it? Why now?\", example: \"What's the revenue impact of this problem per month? And what happens if you don't fix it in the next 90 days?\", duration_sec: 600, required: true },\n    { stage: \"gap_summary\", label: \"Gap Summary\", prompt: \"Summarize the gap with specific numbers. Get verbal agreement.\", example: \"So you're losing ~$30k/month from churn, your goal is to get below 5%, and you've tried 2 tools that didn't work. Right?\", duration_sec: 45, required: true },\n    { stage: \"the_stack\", label: \"The Stack\", prompt: \"Present the Grand Slam Offer: dream outcome + bonuses + guarantee + scarcity. Show the value stack with dollar amounts.\", example: \"Here's the plan: [core] worth $50k + [bonus 1] worth $15k + [bonus 2] worth $10k = $75k value. Your investment: $12k. Guaranteed for 60 days.\", duration_sec: 180, required: true },\n    { stage: \"trial_close\", label: \"Trial Close\", prompt: \"Test close with a conditional.\", example: \"If I can show you how we've done this for 3 companies in your space and the exact plan for you, would you be ready to start this week?\", duration_sec: 20, required: true },\n    { stage: \"objection_handling\", label: \"Objection Handling\", prompt: \"Use LAER: Listen → Acknowledge → Explore → Respond. Isolate the real objection before answering.\", example: \"I hear you on timing. Just so I understand — is it that you need to think about it, or is there a specific concern about the offer itself?\", duration_sec: 240, required: false },\n    { stage: \"close\", label: \"Close\", prompt: \"Give a clear next action. Don't leave it open.\", example: \"Here's what happens next: I send the agreement today, you sign, and we kick off Monday. Does Monday work for the kickoff?\", duration_sec: 30, required: true },\n    { stage: \"post_call\", label: \"Post-Call\", prompt: \"Send a recap + proposal within 2 hours. Set the follow-up call before ending this one.\", example: \"I'll send a recap by 5pm today. Can we reconnect Thursday at 2pm to go over any questions?\", duration_sec: 90, required: true },\n  ],\n  high_ticket: [\n    { stage: \"pre_call\", label: \"Pre-Call Research\", prompt: \"Comprehensive research: financials if available, org chart, strategic priorities, tech stack, recent hires, press releases. Prepare a 1-page brief.\", example: \"I prepared a brief on your current GTM motion based on your last 3 earnings calls and the recent VP of Sales hire.\", duration_sec: 600, required: true },\n    { stage: \"pattern_interrupt\", label: \"Pattern Interrupt\", prompt: \"Demonstrate expertise immediately. Reference something they wouldn't expect you to know.\", example: \"I'll skip the pleasantries — I noticed you're burning $400k/year on 3 tools that overlap. Can I show you what I found?\", duration_sec: 20, required: true },\n    { stage: \"set_frame\", label: \"Set the Frame\", prompt: \"Establish that this is a consultation, not a pitch. You decide if they qualify.\", example: \"This call is about whether there's a fit. I work with a limited number of clients, so I need to make sure the math works for both of us. Let me ask some questions first.\", duration_sec: 30, required: true },\n    { stage: \"discovery\", label: \"Deep Discovery\", prompt: \"Map the full picture: current state, desired state, constraints, stakeholders, timeline, budget. Use the 5 whys.\", example: \"You mentioned churn is the priority. Why is that the priority now vs. 6 months ago? And why is that important to you personally?\", duration_sec: 900, required: true },\n    { stage: \"gap_summary\", label: \"Gap Summary\", prompt: \"Present the gap in terms of money, time, and risk. Get explicit agreement on the cost of inaction.\", example: \"At your current trajectory, you'll lose $1.2M in ARR over the next 12 months. The cost of not fixing this is $1.2M. Do you see it the same way?\", duration_sec: 60, required: true },\n    { stage: \"the_stack\", label: \"The Stack\", prompt: \"Present the Grand Slam Offer with full value stack. Anchor high. Use the value equation: dream × likelihood / (time × effort).\", example: \"The engagement: strategy + implementation + coaching + SLA. Total value: $250k+. Investment: $75k. If we don't hit the agreed KPIs in 90 days, you get a full refund.\", duration_sec: 300, required: true },\n    { stage: \"trial_close\", label: \"Trial Close\", prompt: \"Conditional close based on the specific outcome they described.\", example: \"If we can put together a plan that gets you to <5% churn in 90 days with a money-back guarantee, would you be ready to move forward?\", duration_sec: 25, required: true },\n    { stage: \"objection_handling\", label: \"Objection Handling\", prompt: \"Use the objection isolation technique. Get to the real objection — it's usually risk or trust, not price.\", example: \"Let me make sure I understand: the concern isn't the investment itself, it's whether you can trust the outcome. Is that right? What would make you feel confident?\", duration_sec: 300, required: false },\n    { stage: \"close\", label: \"Close\", prompt: \"Present the close as the logical next step, not a sales push. Offer a choice between two yeses.\", example: \"We can start with the full engagement, or we can do a 2-week paid pilot to prove the model first. Which feels right to you?\", duration_sec: 45, required: true },\n    { stage: \"post_call\", label: \"Post-Call\", prompt: \"Send a written proposal within 24 hours. Include the ROI math. Schedule the follow-up before ending.\", example: \"I'll have a written proposal to you by tomorrow noon with the full ROI breakdown. Can we review it together Wednesday at 10am?\", duration_sec: 120, required: true },\n  ],\n  enterprise: [\n    { stage: \"pre_call\", label: \"Pre-Call Research\", prompt: \"Full account map: decision makers, champions, blockers, budget cycle, procurement process, competitive landscape. Prepare a stakeholder brief.\", example: \"I've mapped your org: [champion] is VP RevOps, [decision maker] is CRO, [blocker] is IT. Budget cycle ends Q3.\", duration_sec: 900, required: true },\n    { stage: \"pattern_interrupt\", label: \"Pattern Interrupt\", prompt: \"Reference a strategic insight about their company or industry that demonstrates deep understanding.\", example: \"Before we start — I'll share that I've worked with 4 companies going through the same platform consolidation you're doing. The #1 thing that kills these projects isn't the tech. It's the change management.\", duration_sec: 30, required: true },\n    { stage: \"set_frame\", label: \"Set the Frame\", prompt: \"Position as a strategic partner, not a vendor. Set expectations for a multi-stakeholder process.\", example: \"This is a discovery call, not a pitch. I need to understand your priorities, your constraints, and your stakeholders. Then I'll tell you if there's a fit and what the next steps would look like.\", duration_sec: 40, required: true },\n    { stage: \"discovery\", label: \"Strategic Discovery\", prompt: \"Map business objectives, current architecture, pain points across teams, success criteria, and decision process. Ask about the champion's personal win.\", example: \"What does success look like for you personally if this project goes well? What does failure look like?\", duration_sec: 1200, required: true },\n    { stage: \"gap_summary\", label: \"Gap Summary\", prompt: \"Summarize the strategic gap, the financial impact, and the risk of inaction. Align on priority level.\", example: \"Your current stack costs $2M/year and creates 40 hours/week of manual reconciliation. The gap is both financial and operational. Is this a top-3 priority for the year?\", duration_sec: 90, required: true },\n    { stage: \"the_stack\", label: \"The Stack\", prompt: \"Present as a strategic partnership, not a transaction. Include implementation, training, SLAs, and success metrics.\", example: \"The partnership: platform + implementation + dedicated CSM + quarterly business reviews + SLA. Total annual value: $5M+ in efficiency. Investment: $300k/year.\", duration_sec: 600, required: true },\n    { stage: \"trial_close\", label: \"Trial Close\", prompt: \"Test commitment to the process, not the deal yet.\", example: \"If the business case checks out and the timeline aligns with your budget cycle, would you be ready to move this to a formal evaluation?\", duration_sec: 30, required: true },\n    { stage: \"objection_handling\", label: \"Objection Handling\", prompt: \"Enterprise objections are usually about risk, procurement, or internal politics. Isolate the real concern and address it with proof and process.\", example: \"I understand the concern about integration risk. We've done 200+ integrations with [similar systems]. Would a reference call with [similar company] help address that?\", duration_sec: 600, required: false },\n    { stage: \"close\", label: \"Close\", prompt: \"Enterprise close = mutual action plan. Get agreement on the next steps with dates and owners.\", example: \"Here's the mutual action plan: Week 1 — technical deep dive. Week 2 — business case. Week 3 — security review. Week 4 — procurement. Can we commit to this timeline?\", duration_sec: 90, required: true },\n    { stage: \"post_call\", label: \"Post-Call\", prompt: \"Send a mutual action plan within 48 hours. Include the stakeholder map, timeline, and success criteria.\", example: \"Here's the MAP we discussed, with dates and owners for each step. I'll follow up with [champion] weekly.\", duration_sec: 180, required: true },\n  ],\n};\n\nconst DEFAULT_OBJECTIONS: ObjectionResponse[] = [\n  { objection: \"It's too expensive\", response: \"Compared to what? The cost of the problem is $X/month. This pays for itself in Y weeks.\", reframe: \"Price vs. cost. The price is one-time. The cost of not solving it is recurring.\" },\n  { objection: \"I need to think about it\", response: \"Of course. Just so I can help you think — what specifically do you need to think about? Is it the fit, the timing, or the investment?\", reframe: \"Think about it = not enough information or not enough trust. Find out which.\" },\n  { objection: \"I need to talk to my partner/team\", response: \"Makes sense. What do you think they'll say? What concerns do you think they'll have?\", reframe: \"If they'll say yes, get them on the phone now. If they'll say no, find out why before they do.\" },\n  { objection: \"We're already using a competitor\", response: \"Great — that means you see the value in solving this. What's working well, and what's not working?\", reframe: \"Competitor presence = validated need. Find the gap, not the fight.\" },\n  { objection: \"Now's not a good time\", response: \"I understand. When would be a good time? And what would need to change between now and then?\", reframe: \"Bad time = low priority. Either the pain isn't big enough or the value isn't clear.\" },\n  { objection: \"Can you send me some info?\", response: \"Happy to. So I send the right thing — are you looking to solve [problem X] or [problem Y]?\", reframe: \"Send me info = brush-off 90% of the time. Refocus on the problem before sending anything.\" },\n  { objection: \"Does it do [feature]?\", response: \"It does. But more importantly — is [feature] something you need to solve [their stated problem]?\", reframe: \"Feature questions = looking for a reason to say no. Redirect to outcomes.\" },\n  { objection: \"What's the ROI?\", response: \"Based on what you told me — [problem] costs you $X/month. We solve it for $Y. ROI is Zx in the first year.\", reframe: \"Have the ROI math ready before they ask. Use their numbers, not yours.\" },\n];\n\nexport function getDefaultSteps(pricePoint: PricePoint): ScriptStep[] {\n  return DEFAULT_STEPS[pricePoint] || DEFAULT_STEPS.mid_ticket;\n}\n\nexport function getDefaultObjections(): ObjectionResponse[] {\n  return DEFAULT_OBJECTIONS;\n}\n\nexport function getStageOrder(): ScriptStage[] {\n  return STAGE_ORDER;\n}\n\n// ── CRUD ──\n\nexport function listScripts(workspaceId?: string): SalesScript[] {\n  const rows = workspaceId\n    ? db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM sales_scripts WHERE workspace_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all(workspaceId)\n    : db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM sales_scripts ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all();\n  return (rows as Record<string, unknown>[]).map(mapScriptRow);\n}\n\nexport function getScript(id: string): SalesScript | null {\n  const row = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM sales_scripts WHERE id = ?`).get(id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n  return row ? mapScriptRow(row) : null;\n}\n\nexport function createScript(data: {\n  workspace_id?: string;\n  name: string;\n  industry?: Industry;\n  price_point?: PricePoint;\n  product_name?: string;\n  product_price?: string;\n}): SalesScript {\n  const id = uid(\"ss_\");\n  const ts = now();\n  const pricePoint = data.price_point || \"mid_ticket\";\n  const steps = getDefaultSteps(pricePoint);\n  const objections = getDefaultObjections();\n  db.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO sales_scripts (id, workspace_id, name, industry, price_point, product_name, product_price, steps, objection_bank, status, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'draft', ?, ?)`,\n  ).run(id, data.workspace_id || \"default\", data.name, data.industry || \"b2b\", pricePoint, data.product_name || \"\", data.product_price || \"\", JSON.stringify(steps), JSON.stringify(objections), ts, ts);\n  return getScript(id)!;\n}\n\nexport function updateScript(id: string, data: Partial<Omit<SalesScript, \"id\" | \"created_at\" | \"updated_at\">>): SalesScript | null {\n  const existing = getScript(id);\n  if (!existing) return null;\n  const fields = Object.keys(data).filter((k) => k !== \"id\" && k !== \"created_at\" && k !== \"updated_at\");\n  if (fields.length === 0) return existing;\n  const setParts: string[] = [];\n  const values: (string | number)[] = [];\n  for (const f of fields) {\n    setParts.push(`${f} = ?`);\n    const val = (data as Record<string, unknown>)[f];\n    values.push(typeof val === \"object\" ? JSON.stringify(val) : (val as string | number));\n  }\n  db.prepare(`UPDATE sales_scripts SET ${setParts.join(\", \")}, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`).run(...values, now(), id);\n  return getScript(id);\n}\n\nexport function deleteScript(id: string): boolean {\n  return db.prepare(`DELETE FROM sales_scripts WHERE id = ?`).run(id).changes > 0;\n}\n\nexport function listRuns(scriptId: string): ScriptRun[] {\n  const rows = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM sales_script_runs WHERE script_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC`).all(scriptId) as Record<string, unknown>[];\n  return rows.map((r) => ({\n    id: String(r.id),\n    script_id: String(r.script_id),\n    contact_name: String(r.contact_name || \"\"),\n    contact_company: String(r.contact_company || \"\"),\n    outcome: String(r.outcome || \"pending\") as ScriptRun[\"outcome\"],\n    notes: String(r.notes || \"\"),\n    deal_value: r.deal_value ? Number(r.deal_value) : null,\n    created_at: Number(r.created_at) || 0,\n  }));\n}\n\nexport function createRun(scriptId: string, data: { contact_name: string; contact_company?: string; deal_value?: number }): string {\n  const id = uid(\"sr_\");\n  db.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO sales_script_runs (id, script_id, contact_name, contact_company, outcome, notes, deal_value, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'pending', '', ?, ?)`,\n  ).run(id, scriptId, data.contact_name, data.contact_company || \"\", data.deal_value || null, now());\n  return id;\n}\n\nexport function updateRun(runId: string, data: { outcome?: ScriptRun[\"outcome\"]; notes?: string; deal_value?: number }): void {\n  const sets: string[] = [];\n  const vals: (string | number)[] = [];\n  if (data.outcome) { sets.push(\"outcome = ?\"); vals.push(data.outcome); }\n  if (data.notes != null) { sets.push(\"notes = ?\"); vals.push(data.notes); }\n  if (data.deal_value != null) { sets.push(\"deal_value = ?\"); vals.push(data.deal_value); }\n  if (sets.length === 0) return;\n  db.prepare(`UPDATE sales_script_runs SET ${sets.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...vals, runId);\n}\n\nfunction mapScriptRow(r: Record<string, unknown>): SalesScript {\n  let steps: ScriptStep[] = [];\n  let objections: ObjectionResponse[] = [];\n  try { steps = JSON.parse(String(r.steps || \"[]\")); } catch { /* ignore */ }\n  try { objections = JSON.parse(String(r.objection_bank || \"[]\")); } catch { /* ignore */ }\n  return {\n    id: String(r.id),\n    workspace_id: String(r.workspace_id || \"default\"),\n    name: String(r.name),\n    industry: String(r.industry || \"b2b\") as Industry,\n    price_point: String(r.price_point || \"mid_ticket\") as PricePoint,\n    product_name: String(r.product_name || \"\"),\n    product_price: String(r.product_price || \"\"),\n    steps,\n    objection_bank: objections,\n    status: String(r.status || \"draft\"),\n    created_at: Number(r.created_at) || 0,\n    updated_at: Number(r.updated_at) || 0,\n  };\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/sales-scripts.ts"
}

{
  "content": "/**\n * Unit Economics — Hormozi's mental math for business profitability.\n *\n * Based on Alex Hormozi's framework from YouTube and Acquisition.com:\n *   - LTV:CAC ratio (target > 3:1)\n *   - Payback period (target < 12 months)\n *   - Gross margin per customer\n *   - Contribution margin (after CAC)\n *   - The \"magic number\" = (Revenue - CAC) / Revenue\n *   - Break-even CAC = LTV × target margin\n *   - Max affordable CAC = LTV / desired LTV:CAC ratio\n */\nimport { db, uid, now } from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nexport interface UnitEconomicsInput {\n  price: number;\n  cogs: number;\n  cac: number;\n  ltv: number;\n  name?: string;\n  workspace_id?: string;\n}\n\nexport interface UnitEconomicsResult {\n  grossMargin: number;\n  grossMarginPct: number;\n  paybackMonths: number;\n  ltvCac: number;\n  contributionMargin: number;\n  contributionMarginPct: number;\n  maxAffordableCac: number;\n  breakEvenCac: number;\n  verdict: string;\n  healthScore: number;\n  levers: { lever: string; value: number; status: \"good\" | \"warning\" | \"danger\"; note: string }[];\n}\n\nexport function calculateUnitEconomics(input: UnitEconomicsInput): UnitEconomicsResult {\n  const price = Math.max(0, input.price);\n  const cogs = Math.max(0, input.cogs);\n  const cac = Math.max(0, input.cac);\n  const ltv = Math.max(0, input.ltv);\n\n  const grossMargin = price - cogs;\n  const grossMarginPct = price > 0 ? (grossMargin / price) * 100 : 0;\n  const paybackMonths = grossMargin > 0 ? cac / grossMargin : Infinity;\n  const ltvCac = cac > 0 ? ltv / cac : Infinity;\n  const contributionMargin = ltv - cac;\n  const contributionMarginPct = ltv > 0 ? (contributionMargin / ltv) * 100 : 0;\n  const maxAffordableCac = ltv / 3;\n  const breakEvenCac = ltv * 0.67;\n\n  const levers: UnitEconomicsResult[\"levers\"] = [\n    {\n      lever: \"Gross Margin %\",\n      value: grossMarginPct,\n      status: grossMarginPct >= 70 ? \"good\" : grossMarginPct >= 40 ? \"warning\" : \"danger\",\n      note: grossMarginPct >= 70 ? \"Healthy SaaS margins\" : grossMarginPct >= 40 ? \"Workable but tight\" : \"Too low — raise price or cut COGS\",\n    },\n    {\n      lever: \"Payback Period (months)\",\n      value: isFinite(paybackMonths) ? paybackMonths : 0,\n      status: paybackMonths <= 6 ? \"good\" : paybackMonths <= 12 ? \"warning\" : \"danger\",\n      note: paybackMonths <= 6 ? \"Fast payback — scale aggressively\" : paybackMonths <= 12 ? \"Acceptable for most businesses\" : \"Too slow — you'll run out of cash before scaling\",\n    },\n    {\n      lever: \"LTV:CAC Ratio\",\n      value: isFinite(ltvCac) ? ltvCac : 0,\n      status: ltvCac >= 3 ? \"good\" : ltvCac >= 1.5 ? \"warning\" : \"danger\",\n      note: ltvCac >= 3 ? \"Healthy — can afford to spend more on acquisition\" : ltvCac >= 1.5 ? \"Marginal — optimize before scaling spend\" : \"Losing money per customer — fix before scaling\",\n    },\n    {\n      lever: \"Contribution Margin\",\n      value: contributionMargin,\n      status: contributionMargin > 0 ? \"good\" : \"danger\",\n      note: contributionMargin > 0 ? \"Each customer adds profit\" : \"Each customer loses money — unsustainable\",\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const healthScore = levers.reduce((sum, l) => {\n    return sum + (l.status === \"good\" ? 25 : l.status === \"warning\" ? 12 : 0);\n  }, 0);\n\n  let verdict: string;\n  if (healthScore >= 90) {\n    verdict = \"Scale. Pour fuel on acquisition. Your unit economics are strong — every dollar of CAC generates healthy return. Increase ad spend, hire more sales, push harder.\";\n  } else if (healthScore >= 60) {\n    verdict = \"Close. One or two levers need work. Fix the weakest metric before scaling spend. Don't pour fuel on a leaky engine.\";\n  } else if (healthScore >= 30) {\n    verdict = \"Fix before scale. You're burning money on every customer. Either raise prices, increase LTV (retention/upsells), or cut CAC. Don't scale until payback < 12 months.\";\n  } else {\n    verdict = \"Critical. You're losing money on every customer. Stop paid acquisition immediately. Fix the offer, fix the pricing, fix the retention. Come back when LTV:CAC > 1.5.\";\n  }\n\n  return {\n    grossMargin,\n    grossMarginPct,\n    paybackMonths: isFinite(paybackMonths) ? paybackMonths : 0,\n    ltvCac: isFinite(ltvCac) ? ltvCac : 0,\n    contributionMargin,\n    contributionMarginPct,\n    maxAffordableCac,\n    breakEvenCac,\n    verdict,\n    healthScore,\n    levers,\n  };\n}\n\nexport function saveUnitEconomics(input: UnitEconomicsInput & { notes?: string }): string {\n  const result = calculateUnitEconomics(input);\n  const id = uid(\"ue_\");\n  const ts = now();\n  db.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO unit_economics (id, workspace_id, name, price, cogs, cac, ltv, gross_margin, payback_months, ltv_cac, contribution_margin, notes, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,\n  ).run(\n    id,\n    input.workspace_id || \"default\",\n    input.name || \"Untitled\",\n    input.price,\n    input.cogs,\n    input.cac,\n    input.ltv,\n    result.grossMargin,\n    result.paybackMonths,\n    result.ltvCac,\n    result.contributionMargin,\n    input.notes || null,\n    ts,\n    ts,\n  );\n  return id;\n}\n\nexport function listUnitEconomics(workspaceId?: string): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {\n  return workspaceId\n    ? db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM unit_economics WHERE workspace_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all(workspaceId)\n    : db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM unit_economics ORDER BY updated_at DESC`).all();\n}",
  "filePath": "/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/unit-economics.ts"
}
